title: Business Operations Specialist/Site Reliability Engineer
description: Role: BizOps Senior Engineer/Site Reliability Engineer Location: Dublin, Ireland Permanent/Fixed Term Full time Employment **Role \& Responsibilities** As a **BizOps Senior Engineer** , you will act as the production readiness steward for versatile Gateway products and integration with other platforms. You will partner with development teams to design, implement, and support services with a focus on operational resilience, automation, and compliance. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Lifecycle Ownership:** Engage in and improve the entire service lifecycle—from design and deployment to operations and continuous improvement. * **Operational Readiness:** Ensure system availability, capacity, performance, monitoring, and self\-healing capabilities are embedded throughout delivery. * **Incident Management:** Practice sustainable incident response, lead blameless postmortems, and optimize Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR). * **Automation \& CI/CD:** * Develop and maintain automation pipelines for certificate renewal, traffic routing, alerting, and compliance reporting using tools like **Ansible, Venafi \& XLR template** . * Support CI/CD pipelines for software promotion and operational gating. * **Reliability Engineering:** Scale systems sustainably through automation and advocate for changes that improve reliability and velocity. * **Compliance \& Risk Management:** Drive initiatives for Safety \& Soundness, PCI compliance, threat/toil reduction, and ITSM defect resolution. * **Monitoring \& Observability:** Implement robust logging, monitoring, and alerting standards to ensure system health and proactive issue detection. Hands\-on experience with **Dynatrace \& Splunk** monitoring tool configuration and alerting. * **Collaboration:** Work with global teams across multiple time zones and mentor junior engineers. * **Continuous Improvement:** Provide feedback loops to development teams on resiliency gaps and operational enhancements. * **Rotational On\-Call \& Flexibility:** * Participate in rotational on\-call support for critical production systems. * Demonstrate flexibility to take on additional responsibilities and ad\-hoc duties as needed to support team and organizational goals. **All About You (Skills \& Qualifications)** * **Experience:** 5\+ years in BizOps, Site Reliability Engineering, or DevOps roles. **Technical Expertise:** * Strong understanding of **NGINX configuration** and **gRPC event\-driven architectures** . * Proficiency in DevOps tools: **Chef, Jenkins, Groovy, shell scripting, Bitbucket, Git, Ansible, XLR** . * Experience with **AWS infrastructure** , secure access practices, and cloud\-native deployments. **Security \& Compliance:** * Awareness of certificate lifecycle management, mutual TLS, SSL handshake, SSH keys, encryption standards. * Familiarity with ITSM processes, compliance frameworks, and incident management. **Networking \& Systems:** * Knowledge of client\-server relationships, network layers (L1–L7\), load balancers ( **BIG\-IP F5** ), and application firewalls. * Ability to analyze stack traces, TCP dumps, heap/thread dumps, and perform OS\-level troubleshooting. **Authentication \& Authorization:** * Intermediate understanding of **Active Directory, SAML, LTPA, SSO, OAuth** . **Soft Skills:** * Strong documentation and communication skills. * Ability to collaborate across cross\-functional teams and mentor junior resources. **Preferred Experience:** * Building self\-healing systems and operational resiliency frameworks. * Implementing observability solutions for distributed systems. * Driving DevOps transformation and automation best practices in large\-scale environments.

title: Business and Data Analyst Graduate
description: **Company Description** PartnerRe is a leading, privately owned, multi\-line global reinsurer with a reputation of financial stability and strength, and a commitment to rebuilding businesses and communities after risk events around the world. Our mission is to continue to be a financially stable and predictable business partner, supporting our clients with solutions tailored to their needs, delivering superior returns to our owner, providing rewarding opportunities for our people and making a positive contribution to society. To achieve this, we live by our values of Integrity, Performance, Collaboration, Straightforward Communication and Respect and Care. Core to The PartnerRe Experience is our global nature. No matter where you sit in the company, you are part of something bigger. Our teams represent our truly international mindset, forging connections across office locations and all levels of the organization and building a network of mutual success. The result is a day\-to\-day experience that will allow you to: **Shape your own career** **Grow in a supportive environment** **Connect with a community of experts** **Make an impact** View Home \- PartnerRe Experience e\-book to find out more about what it’s like to work at PartnerRe. **We are always looking for bright, proactive individuals to join our team!** Please follow our Careers page for all updates on new positions. We look forward to receiving your application. **PartnerRe is an equal opportunities employer.** Please join our company LinkedIn Page for all updates on new positions that are coming live. We are looking to hire an Intern to join our Planning \& Performance Management (PPM) team in Dublin on a **nine\-month fixed\-term contract** . In this role you will split your time between two distinct teams: Underwriting Reporting \& Analysis and Underwriting Management Information. As such, your tasks will be quite varied and may include: * Supporting our Business Unit Analysts in assessing our financial counterparty risk * Analyzing, testing reports and reworking existing dashboards to improve efficiency * Collaborating with dedicated team members and working on commutation requests (when contractual obligations are to be discharged) * Participating in a company\-wide Power BI community * Working with data tools to build data models * Providing suggestions to automate/improve manual processes and implementing automation solutions where necessary **About The Team** The PPM team currently consists of 19 experienced members and works closely with several other teams, such as our Underwriting, Operations, Actuarial and IT teams. Within Underwriting Reporting \& Analysis and Underwriting Management Information you will interact with 9 individuals spread across 3 locations (Stamford, USA; Paris, France; Zurich, Switzerland) and representing 7 different nationalities. **Key Skills** * English (business fluency) * Inquisitive with a learning mindset * Experience or interest in the insurance sector * Understanding of financial topics such as Profit \& Loss and Balance Sheet analysis * Familiarity with Microsoft Excel / Power Query / Power BI (or similar tools) * High\-level understanding of relational database concepts / data modelling **About You** You are a curious and analytical person who has a desire to learn about the workings of the reinsurance industry whilst applying and developing your skills. You wish to work in a dynamic and culturally diverse environment with colleagues possessing a wide and varied range of backgrounds. You are entitled to work in Dublin. **Additional Information** : PartnerRe provides a working environment in which diversity is valued, where all employees feel welcome, respected, and empowered to perform at their best. We invite you to learn more about D\&I at PartnerRe. Thank you, PartnerRe Hiring Team

safety / risk assessments as stipulated in the Childcare Regulations * Effective people and business management skills with the confidence and skills to cover all business aspects of the role * Excellent knowledge of and ability to implement child protection policies in line with Children First requirements and guidelines * Lead on the development and evaluation of programmes and activities conducive to culturally sensitive learning environments **Required Qualifications** Candidates must have the following: * A minimum of FETAC / QQI Level 6 in Early Childhood Care and Education or equivalent * A minimum of five years in the childcare field and at least two year of these in a management role within the sector * A strong passion for the needs and development of the individual child * Full clean EU driver’s licence and have access to a vehicle **Candidates Must Be Able To Demonstrate The Following** * Ability to liaise with parents, guardians and families and build parental partnership * Ability to line manage and supervise a team, supporting their continuous personal development * Communicate effectively in a clear and professional manner at all times * Manage and project occupancy levels * Ability to meet targets and deadlines and manage finances within an allocated budget * Have a full understanding off all guidelines as set out by TUSLA. Applicants must be willing to provide written references and be Garda Vetted by the company (Police Clearance will also be required if the applicant have lived outside of Ireland for more than 6 consecutive months since over the age of 18\). Proof of eligibility to work in the EU must be provided.

title: Civil Site Supervisor
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** The **Civil Site Supervisor** will play a key role in managing the daily operations of multiple civil crews working across various sites. This position requires excellent coordination, communication, and organisational skills to ensure all works are carried out safely, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * Responsible for running numerous projects within urban and rural environments. * Liaising and corresponding with Clients and Client Representatives to enable the successful delivery of projects and building of relationships. * Reviewing drawings with a high level of detail. * Amending designs in line with feedback received. * Create an environment of leadership within the project site team. * Liaising with Contracts Manager and Site Agents to manage work and site resource requirements. * Ensuring customers' expectations are fulfilled whilst building confidence and trust. * Interrogating \& continuously improving project processes. * Apply new technology in an innovative way to create new services and streamline delivery processes. * Mentor designers and identify training needs for junior site team. * Monitor progress of site work, ensuring work is completed on time and within budget. * Planning and overseeing the delivery of materials and manage any discrepancies where appropriate. * Ensuring that all work complies with client requirements and safety legislation alongside other legal requirements. * Responsible for overseeing the traffic management as well as health and safety on\-site and ensuring all safety checks are carried out. * Liaise with Local Authorities with the inspection of footway conditions prior and post installation works. * Liaise with the workflow department regarding the programming and scheduling of work. * Provide Clients with a status report and act as first point of contact for members of the public and sub\-contractors. * Professionally handle complaints received from customers regarding carried out work. * Collaborate with clients to enable them to maximise the value of their infrastructure. * Quality control of works. * Manage required administrative tasks efficiently and effectively. * Ad hoc tasks as required. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Full clean driver's license essential. * A qualification in engineering or equivalent is desirable. * Civils, drainage, or utilities (water \& electricity) experience. * Previous experience and the ability to read designs. * Commercially aware of projects costs. * Experience with site activities and multiple teams. * Good knowledge of HSQE requirements. * Good knowledge of ground support systems. * Computer literate. * Confident, motivated and can work independently as well as part of a team. * Ability to work under pressure with strict deadlines. * Time management abilities to meet targets. * Good communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. * Integrity and respect for confidentiality and privacy. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"*. Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Clinical Specialist Sonographer (Paediatrics & DDH)
description: **Job Description** **Position Summary:** The Paediatric Clinical Specialist Sonographer will deliver a high\-quality, child\-centred ultrasound service across Medica Diagnostics Ireland sites. Working autonomously within Medica’s clinical governance framework and agreed protocols, the post holder will independently undertake a wide range of paediatric ultrasound examinations and produce accurate, comprehensive provisional ultrasound reports to support timely clinical decision\-making. The role carries specialist responsibility for maintaining excellence in paediatric imaging practice, including referral vetting, oversight of ultrasound equipment performance, and the consistent application of quality assurance measures. The post holder will contribute to protocol development and review, clinical audit, service evaluation, and continuous improvement activity, while acting as a key point of escalation for complex clinical, operational, or equipment\-related issues within the paediatric ultrasound service. As a senior clinical practitioner, the post holder will also play a central role in education, training, and mentorship. This includes supporting the development of trainee and junior sonographers, student radiographers, and wider imaging colleagues through formal teaching, supervision, and day\-to\-day professional guidance. They will help foster a positive learning culture that promotes clinical excellence, professional growth, and safe paediatric practice. The successful candidate will demonstrate flexibility and leadership in supporting service development, cross\-site working, and future expansion of paediatric ultrasound provision, ensuring delivery remains aligned with Medica Diagnostics Ireland’s strategic and operational priorities. **Key Responsibilities** **Clinical Responsibilities** * To be responsible for independently performing a wide range of high\-quality paediatric diagnostic ultrasound examinations in accordance with Medica Diagnostics Ireland procedures, policies, and clinical protocols. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of clinical and technical developments in paediatric sonography, ensuring evidence\-based practice is reflected in scanning protocols and contributing to the development, review, and refinement of local examination techniques and service standards. * Vet ultrasound referrals to ensure clinical appropriateness and justification in line with professional, legislative, and local guidance. Clarify referral details where required and prioritise patients appropriately according to clinical urgency to support safe and timely care. * Apply advanced clinical judgement and specialist paediatric ultrasound expertise in image acquisition, interpretation, and reporting. Produce high\-quality preliminary ultrasound reports, as locally agreed, and escalate urgent, unexpected, or significant findings without delay to support safe patient management. * Maintain accurate, timely, and complete patient records using Medica’s RIS/PACS and associated clinical systems. Ensure all examinations are correctly annotated, documented, and archived in accordance with organisational standards, professional requirements, and data protection legislation. * Adhere to all Medica Diagnostics Ireland policies and procedures relevant to clinical practice, including safeguarding, infection prevention and control, consent, chaperoning, health and safety, clinical record\-keeping, and communication protocols. * Practise within the limits of personal competence and scope of practice. Seek advice, second opinions, or escalate appropriately to the Head of Ultrasound Services, senior clinical colleagues, or reporting radiologists where clinical complexity, uncertainty, or risk requires. * Carry out daily equipment quality checks and basic troubleshooting of ultrasound systems, ensuring equipment remains safe and fit for purpose. Promptly report faults or malfunctions to the Head of Ultrasound Services and vendor support services and communicate any potential service impact to relevant operational leads. * Ensure a child\-centred, compassionate, and professional approach, adapting communication and examination techniques to meet the needs of infants, children, young people, and their families or carers. * Act as a clinical specialist resource within paediatric ultrasound, supporting safe decision\-making, consistent practice, and high standards of care across the service. **Quality, Education and Training** * Contribute to Medica Diagnostics Ireland’s clinical audit and quality assurance activities, including peer review, image quality checks, and documentation review, supporting high standards in paediatric ultrasound practice. * Support the development and review of paediatric ultrasound protocols, quality control processes, and documentation standards to promote consistency, safety, and best practice across the service. * Participate in clinical governance activities, including audit feedback, incident review, and service improvement initiatives, helping to promote a culture of learning, accountability, and continuous improvement. * Report incidents, near misses, and patient safety concerns promptly through the appropriate reporting systems, supporting effective risk management and safe service delivery. * Provide day\-to\-day guidance and support to trainee and junior sonographers and other relevant staff, contributing to their development and helping to foster a positive learning environment. * Contribute to shared learning through case discussion, reflective practice, and participation in internal education and service development activities. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge and skills in paediatric ultrasound through continuing professional development, mandatory training, and participation in relevant internal and external learning opportunities. * Promote clinical excellence, professional development, and high\-quality child\-centred care across the paediatric ultrasound service. **Professional Conduct And Interpersonal Responsibilities** * Maintain effective, respectful communication with colleagues, radiologists, administrative teams, and non\-radiographic staff across all clinical sites to ensure seamless service delivery. * Adhere to Medica’s Communication Policy and guidelines for operational updates, issue reporting, and MDT coordination. Ensure timely and accurate information flow between sonographers, radiologists, referrers, and clinic staff. * Escalate any issues related to the roster or scheduling to Media Operations manager. * Report complaints or service concerns through the appropriate quality management system, ensuring the Head of Ultrasound is informed in a timely manner. * Represent and promote the Medica brand within clinics through professional behaviour, a collaborative attitude, and a commitment to excellence. * Uphold the highest standards of professionalism by treating every patient with courtesy, empathy, efficiency, and confidentiality, delivering a safe, respectful, and patient\-centred service at all times. **Job Requirements** **Qualifications:** * BSc Diagnostics Radiography * MSc in Medical Ultrasound or PgD in Medical Ultrasound * CORU registered **Essential Skills** * 3 years’ experience in acute paediatric radiology setting * Communication * Patient care * Technical Competency * Attention to detail * Teamwork * Time management **Desirable Skills** * Problem solving * Adaptability * Mentorship and leadership * Audit and Research

title: Company Secretary Manager
description: An established international financial services organisation within the funds industry is seeking to appoint a Senior Manager – Company Secretarial to support a growing portfolio of Irish regulated entities and fund structures. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced company secretarial professional to step into a leadership role, managing client relationships, overseeing board governance processes and supporting the development of a high\-performing team. The successful candidate will manage a portfolio of Irish fund entities while leading and mentoring members of the company secretarial team. The role involves regular interaction with directors, clients and key stakeholders to ensure high standards of governance and regulatory compliance. **Key Responsbilities** * Managing a portfolio of Irish entities including preparation of board packs, agendas and minutes for board and committee meetings. * Acting as a key point of contact for directors, clients and internal stakeholders. * Ensuring timely follow\-up on matters arising from board meetings and maintaining strong communication with clients. * Ensuring compliance with applicable corporate governance and company law requirements. * Managing regulatory filings and statutory obligations for Irish entities. * Monitoring developments in corporate governance and company law and advising boards on relevant obligations. * Leading and mentoring junior team members and supporting the development of the company secretarial function. * Reviewing and improving operational procedures and internal governance processes. * Monitoring regulatory developments affecting the funds industry and assessing their impact on the function. **Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in law, business, accounting or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years’ experience in a company secretarial role within financial services, ideally within the funds industry. * Strong knowledge of Irish funds regulation and corporate governance requirements. * Demonstrated experience in team leadership or people management. * Experience supporting boards and maintaining relationships with senior stakeholders. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Compliance Analyst
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way Compliance Analyst (12 Months Fixed Term Contract) Technology at Primark Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Pension,27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Do as a** **Compliance Analyst** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Develop a strong understanding of the current system and application landscape to support future solution design. * Assess practices and policies against Primark’s compliance and security standards, identifying and prioritising any gaps with vendors and product teams. * Collaborate with security teams, vendors, and developers to initiate, analyse, and action outcomes from vulnerability scans. * Represent security, compliance, and regulatory needs within project requirements and ensure they are championed across product teams. * Manage compliance activities, including cookie compliance and security scanning across the digital landscape, ensuring timely remediation and risk mitigation. * Provide regular reporting on compliance and security risks while supporting multiple initiatives in parallel. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Strong experience with digital cookie consent management tools, online security, and compliance initiatives. * Ability to analyse vulnerability scans and translate findings into actionable remediation steps. * Solid functional and technical understanding of digital systems, including websites, platforms, and related operations. * Excellent communication skills with the ability to build strong internal and external relationships, including with senior stakeholders. * Strong planning and organisational skills, with the ability to manage workloads, prioritise tasks, and meet agreed timelines. * Knowledge of security and compliance requirements, supported by relevant education (degree or equivalent). Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-5745

title: Consultant
description: **What’s in it for me?** * Competitive salary and compensation structure * Generous paid time off and holiday schedule * Frequent firm\-wide social events and activities * Excellent environment for learning and growth * Further benefits, depending on location **About The Role** Penta is seeking a Consultant to join our Dublin team in this position you will play a role in delivering strategic communications, media, political and public affairs programmes across a range of client accounts. The role involves providing strategic advice and professional support to clients, leading stakeholder engagement and campaign delivery, managing account teams, and contributing to business development. This is a client\-facing role within a fast\-paced consultancy environment. **Principal Responsibilities** * Play a senior role on a number of accounts and projects. * Demonstrate leadership skills while managing account teams to deliver on goals, objectives and needs of clients. * Monitor and anticipate revenues and profitability of client accounts in support of the Managing Director. * Maintain and share a strong understanding of clients’ core sectors in order to provide strategic counsel on strategies, messaging and tactics, and anticipate issues that could arise. * Contribute to and cultivate business development opportunities. * Assist in targeted marketing of the company to corporate audiences. * Ensure effective and efficient internal communications, keeping senior team members abreast of ongoing projects, issues, or risks. **General Responsibilities:** * Generate and develop regular ideas for clients. Ensure all activities meet / exceed client expectations. * Ensure regular (internal and external) planning meetings take place with all clients and foster close working relationships. * Ensure team members maintain company standards for planning, reporting and quality of service. Manage team members, ensuring daily and weekly targets are met. * Manage collaborative projects across the team displaying excellent written and oral communication; comprehensive understanding of data analysis; deep knowledge of strategic communications market as well as political and policymaking environment in Ireland. * Assist in the development and mentoring of junior staff members. * Be a proactive member of the Penta team and be part of creating a happy and supportive working environment for team members. * Perform any other duties that are assigned to you by your manager and for which you have been provided with reasonable knowledge and training in respect of such responsibilities. **About You** * Be highly motivated, dynamic, and driven to become a key player in our integrated, international service offering with a focus on Ireland and the EU. * Have 3\-5 years of experience in public affairs and communications, specifically focused on Ireland and EU affairs. * Have a strong understanding of the policy framework and how the Irish government and EU institutions operate, as well as key legislative and regulatory processes. * Demonstrate the ability to manage a team of consultants, working to meet tight deadlines and exceed high client expectations. * Possess a strategic and curious mindset, always looking ahead and identifying future opportunities. * Be committed to the highest professional standards, consistently going above and beyond client expectations. * Show flexibility and collaborative working skills, effectively managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment, with a keen attention to detail. * Possess first\-rate analytical and communication skills, ensuring impactful and insightful engagement with clients and stakeholders across Ireland and the EU. **About Us** Penta Group is a global company providing data\-driven solutions to help clients achieve their objectives in an increasingly complex stakeholder environment. We provide cutting\-edge intelligence products to help clients understand what their stakeholders think, see, and hear, and then leverage that intelligence to develop and execute effective, measurable strategies. Our global team brings decades of experience in business, government, communications, research, data science, and media. Our clients include companies across a variety of industries, including technology, financial services, energy, healthcare, and more. Penta has offices in New York, London, Washington, DC, San Francisco, Dublin, London, Brussels, Singapore, and Hong Kong.

title: Corporate Tax FDI Manager
description: **Deloitte is the biggest professional services Firm in the world and making an impact is more than just what we do: it’s why we’re here. We’re driven to create positive progress for our clients, community, people, and the planet. This sense of purpose inspires us to work to the highest standards, to tackle the challenges that matter.** The tax landscape is constantly evolving and has experienced unprecedented change in recent years. Our Deloitte experts partner with our clients to navigate their tax complexities and support their business ambitions. Our tax leaders combine strategic commercial insight and intricate tax knowledge to support our client’s strategic decisions. We share our experience, reimagine operating models, and leverage cutting\-edge technology in combination with innovative ideas and our global network of professionals, to make an impact that matters to our clients. Why you'll enjoy this new opportunity Join Deloitte’s dynamic Corporate and International Tax Advisory team at a pivotal time of growth and increased demand. This role offers the chance to lead and supervise diverse, high\-impact tax advisory projects across multiple geographies, including cutting\-edge areas such as IP restructuring, Pillar Two initiatives, business model optimisation, and M\&A transactions. You will work alongside experienced managers, directors, and partners, contributing directly to client success and the expansion of the practice. This position provides excellent leadership opportunities, exposure to global projects, and a platform to develop your career within a supportive and forward\-thinking environment. **Key skills and prior experience that align well with this position** * AITI/CTC qualified, ideally complemented by ACA qualification, with proven corporate and international tax advisory experience. * Strong technical knowledge of corporate tax, with experience advising on complex tax advisory projects. * Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively and work collaboratively within high\-performing teams. * Experience in sectors such as pharmaceuticals, technology, FinTech, or energy is advantageous, alongside exposure to global advisory projects. * Strong commercial awareness and a proactive approach to sustaining and growing client relationships. **Success in the Role** * Deliver high\-quality tax advisory services to a portfolio of corporate and international clients, ensuring technical accuracy and commercial relevance. * Lead and supervise project teams, managing day\-to\-day activities and supporting the smooth running of the business unit. * Contribute to business development efforts by identifying opportunities, supporting proposals, and winning new client engagements. * Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and high performance. **What type of work will I be doing?** * Providing expert tax advice and preparing detailed technical memorandums to support advisory projects * Leading and supervising teams on complex global advisory transactions, including IP restructuring, Pillar Two projects, and M\&A * Managing client relationships and contributing to business growth initiatives * Coaching and developing trainees and senior team members, and collaborating with partners and directors to ensure effective project delivery **Who is the Hiring Manager?** The hiring manager is an experienced tax professional with deep expertise in corporate and international tax advisory. They lead with a collaborative and empowering style, valuing integrity, open communication, and professional development. They encourage innovation and teamwork, supporting their teams to deliver exceptional client service while fostering a positive and inclusive work environment. **Where is this role based?** This role is based in one of Deloitte’s offices in Dublin, Cork or Limerick offering a flexible working model that includes hybrid arrangements. For more details on our working environment, please visit the Deloitte Works webpage. **Rewards And Benefits Of Working At Deloitte Ireland** At Deloitte, your reward is competitive and supports our purpose of helping you never stop growing. We believe in investing in our people's personal and professional development and empowering their work\-life balance. We offer a range of supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including health and well\-being, pension, savings, learning \& development and enhanced leave options. You'll also be part of an inclusive culture that encourages you to bring your full self to work every day. For more, review our Rewards \& Benefits webpage. *If you believe that you meet most of the criteria above, we encourage you to apply. Deloitte is an equal opportunities employer and will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process.* **What We Offer** *Your reward at Deloitte is competitive, supports our purpose and enables our people to never stop growing. Your reward at Deloitte is not just your salary, we believe in investing in our people's personal and professional development and empowering their work\-life balance. Our ambition is to achieve gender equality and enable continuous growth. We offer supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including health and well\-being, pension, savings, training, coaching* *and enhanced leave options am* *ong others.* *Keep on being you. Bring your full, absolute self to work, every day. Explore, question and collaborate. Stretch your thinking, while building a career that inspires and energises you. And, whatever motivates you, keep gro* *wing both professionally and personally – because when you make an impact that matters, we do too.*

title: Counter Associate "The Ordinary" Arnotts
description: **WHAT ON EARTH IS DECIEM?** We are a collective of disrupters and doers, of creatives and scientists, with a mission to reimagine the world of beauty. Since inception, our vertically integrated structure, including our own laboratories, manufacturing, retail stores, and brand infrastructure, have enabled us to rapidly identify opportunities and deliver quality. Home to The Ordinary, NIOD, AVESTAN, and other emerging brands, our goal is to create with purpose. We were founded on the principle of doing everything that others don’t. Because normal gets you nowhere. We are DECIEM, The Abnormal Beauty Company. We are growing rapidly and we’re looking for someone who shares this vision and wants to grow with us! **WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR** We are looking for a flexible customer service\-oriented Sales Assistant known at DECIEM as Counter Associate, to join our " **extraOrdinary"** team in **Arnotts, Dublin** to deliver memorable experiences to our customers \<3 Experience in the **Retail, Department Stores, and luxury industry** is highly desirable and ideally, within the **Fragrance, Beauty, Fashion** , and Luxury Goods sectors. * This is a 24\-hour\-a\-week position on a part\-time basis **WHAT YOU'D BE DOING IN THIS ROLE** * Greet all customers who enter the store with enthusiasm and professionalism reflecting DECIEM’s vision through excellent service * Determine the customer’s needs and suggest relevant products by listening to the customer and sharing the product knowledge acquired during brand training * Ensure standards for quality, customer service, and health and safety are met * Adhere to the DECIEM experience and deliver unparalleled customer service. Educate customers about the world of Beauty and Skin Care, DECIEM brands, and products * Manage customer feedback and queries * Assist in maintaining counter standards of cleanliness and organization * Be aware of and assist in maintaining DECIEM’s merchandising concepts * Ensure tidiness standards are always respected and maintained * Conduct product refunds and address customer issues in a positive manner * Responsible for building meaningful relationships with department managers and clients. High standard communication. **SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS NEEDED TO GET THE JOB DONE** - Comfortable in engaging customers and applying products to the customer while providing consultations - Ability to multi\-task, while providing professional and outstanding customer service Desire to spread brand awareness - Effective time management and communication skills. **WHAT YOU'LL BE GAINING BY JOINING OUR TEAM** There’s a lot of good stuff that comes along with being a DECIEM team member. Here’s a few of our favourite perks and benefits, in no particular order… Generous Vacation \& Personal Days, plus additional time off for volunteering in your community, voting, peaceful protesting, celebrating your birthday, and more. 6 months of paid time off for new parents (inclusive of all genders). Unlimited access to an Employee Assistance Program that includes mental health care, mindfulness programs, and more. Access to Development Grants \& a LinkedIn Learning membership to help you keep growing and learning. First dibs on new products. A generous discount on DECIEM products for you, your family, and your friends. **BUILDING GROWTH TO POWER GOOD** We are a people\-first company that lives by our core values to… * ✨ Defy Convention. * ✨ Make it real. * ✨ Play in the grey. * ✨ Build Bridges. * ✨ Be the rabbit. These values continue to guide us in all that we do. Along the way, we’ve been honoured to win some pretty incredible awards, including a CEW Achiever Award, Best Skincare Brand at Sunday Times Style Beauty Awards, and Most Popular Sustainable Brand by The Cosmetify Index Global. We’re still blushing! **SUSTAINABILITY AND SOCIAL IMPACT** DECIEM’s objective is to build growth and do good things while prioritizing people, animals, and the Earth. Our Sustainability strategy is focused on fighting climate change, improved waste management, responsible packaging, responsible sourcing and product lifecycle, and water conservation. In 2020, DECIEM established the Good Fund to support both large and grassroots charities in response to global events. **CREATING A HUMAN WORLD OF BEAUTY** DECIEM is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) by creating meaningful, measurable change in the lives of our team members and in the communities where we operate. Achieving equality is critical to DECIEM and our future. You can learn more about our DEI commitment in our open letter, " DECIEM is an equal opportunity employer. We prohibit discrimination based on age, colour, disability, national origin, race, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, and any other legally protected class in accordance with applicable federal, provincial and local laws. We are also committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible workplace. If you are contacted to be part of our recruitment process and require accommodation, please let us know. **WANT TO JOIN US?** Thank you for reading the whole thing! If you liked what you heard, kindly send us your resume. We really appreciate your effort in applying for this position. However, only those who have been shortlisted for interviews will be contacted. \<3 \#SP

title: Data Analyst
description: As a Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in strengthening data governance, analytics, and oversight across Barclays Europe. You will partner with business, risk, and technology stakeholders to deliver high\-quality data insight, support regulatory and control requirements, and enable strategic decision\-making. To be successful as a Data Analyst, you will have: * Extensive experience in data analysis, data governance, and data management within financial services. * The ability to work within risk, control, or regulatory\-driven environments. * An excellent understanding of data quality, controls, lineage, and metadata concepts. * A background of senior stakeholder management, coupled with excellent communication skills. You may be assessed on the key critical skills relevant for success in role, such as risk and controls, change and transformation, business acumen strategic thinking and digital and technology, as well as job\-specific technical skills. This role will be located at our Dublin office. **Purpose of the role** To enable data\-driven strategic and operational decision making through extracting actionable insights from large datasets, performing statistical and advanced analytics to uncover trends and patterns, and presenting findings through clear visualisations and reports. **Accountabilities** * Investigation and analysis of data issues related to quality, lineage, controls, and authoritative source identification, documenting data sources, methodologies, and quality findings with recommendations for improvement. * Designing and building data pipelines to automate data movement and processing. * Apply advanced analytical techniques to large datasets to uncover trends and correlations, develop validated logical data models, and translate insights into actionable business recommendations that drive operational and process improvements, leveraging machine learning/AI. * Through data\-driven analysis, translate analytical findings into actionable business recommendations, identifying opportunities for operational and process improvements. * Design and create interactive dashboards and visual reports using applicable tools and automate reporting processes for regular and ad\-hoc stakeholder needs. **Assistant Vice President Expectations** * To advise and influence decision making, contribute to policy development and take responsibility for operational effectiveness. Collaborate closely with other functions/ business divisions. * Lead a team performing complex tasks, using well developed professional knowledge and skills to deliver on work that impacts the whole business function. Set objectives and coach employees in pursuit of those objectives, appraisal of performance relative to objectives and determination of reward outcomes * If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others. * OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes. * Consult on complex issues; providing advice to People Leaders to support the resolution of escalated issues. * Identify ways to mitigate risk and developing new policies/procedures in support of the control and governance agenda. * Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work done. * Perform work that is closely related to that of other areas, which requires understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub\-function. * Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy. * Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively. * Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience. * Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

title: Dietitian - Nutrition Development Specialist
description: **About Abbott** Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life\-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals and branded generic medicines. Our 107,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. **Abbott Ireland Commercial Liffey Valley Dublin** Opened in 1946, this operation, based in Liffey Valley Dublin was the first Abbott business unit to be established in Ireland. This business is responsible for the sales, marketing and distribution of a wide range of diabetes, diagnostic, and nutritional products in Ireland **This is a 9\-month contract, full time job Monday to Friday, office based.** **Primary Function** The Nutrition Development Specialist will support Nutrition Services, marketing and regulatory team, through providing Dietetic support and medical back up for reimbursement submissions, for new campaigns, product launches and training. This role will be based in our Liffey Valley office reporting directly into the Commercial Lead. **Education / Experience \& Background** * Registered dietitian (with CORU) with 1\-2 years of clinical experience * Excellent knowledge of adult nutrition support – paediatric nutrition support * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * Excellent written and spoken English and communication skills * Good organisational and time management skills. * Adaptable and able to work cross\-functionally. * Competent use of Microsoft Office. * Maintains confidentiality and has a professional manner. **Main Responsibilities** * Provide clinical Support to Regulatory specialist in preparation of clinical trial submissions to the GMS * Maintain and expand nutrition knowledge of the Abbott Nutrition team through training and CPD development * Provide a scientific Medical Nutrition service for marketed products, ensuring it is promoted in a professional manner and is delivered in line with contractual requirements. To work collaboratively with the marketing team and other support functions to support product positioning and claims development. * Develop e\-learning modules and nutrition related materials for supporting training needs of HCP’s * Deliver education and training to internal and external customers. * Maintain corporate image by representing Abbott Nutrition at scientific meetings and exhibitions. * Provide support to Commercial Lead for onboarding Graduate Dietitian * Along with Commercial Lead create a plan for dietetic student training (approx. 4 students per year) **What We Offer** Attractive compensation package that includes **competitive pay and structure Bonus**, as well as benefits such as * Private Health Insurance for you and your family * Occupational Pension Scheme with an excellence contribution rate * Bonus Target * Life assurance * Career Development * Growing business plus access to many more benefits Connect with us at www.abbott.com, on LinkedIn at www.linkedin.com/company/abbott\-/, on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Abbott and on Twitter @AbbottNews and @AbbottGlobal.

title: Ecommerce & Omnichannel Marketing Coordinator
description: **Contract Type:** Full time, permanent **Location:** M7 Business Park, Naas, Co Kildare **Hours:** 37\.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Flexibility in working hours may be required to meet the needs of the organisation. **Department:** Business Development **Reports to:** Omnichannel Marketing \& E\-commerce Manager **Closing date** : 9th April 2026 **Role Overview** The E\-commerce \& Omnichannel Marketing Coordinator will support the delivery and growth of Vision Ireland's online retail operations across Shopify and third\-party marketplaces. This role is suited to a recent graduate or early\-career professional looking to build practical experience in e\-commerce, digital marketing, and online trading. The role is primarily focused on online trading and product performance, with additional responsibility for supporting digital marketing execution. The successful candidate will contribute to product listings, website merchandising, campaign support, and performance tracking, while developing a strong understanding of what drives online sales and customer engagement. **Key Responsibilities** **E\-commerce \& Trading (70%)** * Create, optimise, and publish product listings across Shopify and resale platforms (eBay, Depop, Vinted, Vestiaire) * Ensure all listings are accurate, complete, and aligned with best practice (titles, descriptions, images, pricing, and attributes) * Maintain consistency of product data across all platforms * Support inventory updates, stock flow, and bulk uploads * Review and improve existing listings to enhance visibility and performance * Assist with website merchandising, including collections, categories, and featured products * Monitor product performance and highlight slow\-moving or high\-performing items * Support pricing updates, promotions, and relisting of older inventory **Digital Marketing Support (30%)** * Assist in the execution of email campaigns and relevant marketing content * Support product selection and coordination for campaigns * Ensure alignment between marketing activity and website content * Implement basic SEO tasks such as meta descriptions and image alt text * Support campaign setup, QA, and scheduling **Marketplace \& Platform Support** * Apply platform\-specific best practices to improve listing visibility and performance * Support the use of marketplace tools such as promotions, offers, and relisting * Monitor basic performance KPIs including views, clicks, and sales * Maintain an active and relevant product catalogue across all channels **Customer Experience \& Website Support** * Review product pages and identify issues affecting usability or clarity * Support improvements to site navigation and product presentation * Assist with customer queries where required * Ensure content is clear, accurate, and accessible **Reporting** * Support weekly reporting on sales and product performance * Track key metrics such as sales, conversion rate, and stock movement * Highlight trends and opportunities for improvement * Maintain simple trackers using Excel and platform analytics tools *This is not an exhaustive list of duties. The successful candidate must be open to flexibility in their duties in line with the needs of the organisation.* **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Degree or Professional qualifications in Business, Marketing, Digital, Retail, or a related field * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Interest in e\-commerce, retail, or fashion * Good working knowledge of Excel or similar tools * Strong communication skills and willingness to learn **Desirable** * Experience with Shopify or similar e\-commerce platforms * Familiarity with resale marketplaces (eBay, Depop, Vinted, Vestiaire) * Basic understanding of digital marketing or SEO * Previous retail or customer\-facing experience **What This Role Offers** * Hands\-on experience across multiple e\-commerce platforms * Exposure to digital trading, merchandising, and marketing * Opportunity to develop commercial awareness and analytical skills * A clear pathway into a more senior e\-commerce or digital marketing role **Success Measures** Success in this role will be measured by: * Accuracy and quality of product listings * Volume and efficiency of product uploads * Improvements in product visibility and engagement * Contribution to overall online sales performance * Ability to take ownership of tasks and work independently **Further Information for Candidates:** Vision Ireland reserve the right to close this competition early should an adequate number of applications be received. All applicants must submit a current CV. Candidates must be eligible to work full\-time in Ireland. Further information about Vision Ireland can be found on our website www.vi.ie Informal enquiries to recruitment@vi.ie Vision Ireland is an equal opportunities employer. **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** Vision Ireland is committed to creating an inclusive environment where diversity is celebrated, and everyone is afforded equality of opportunity. It is our policy to recognise people as a key resource required for successful attainment of the organisation's mission. In support of this, it is important to remember that differences between people, whether devised from their different backgrounds and personalities, cultures and/or their different abilities, can be a source of strength to the organisation. We welcome, encourage, and embrace people of all backgrounds, to include those with disabilities. Accessibility plays a huge part of ensuring that all our employees and service users can access our systems and services with ease and respect, in a supportive environment, \- enabling all to effectively engage our services and achieve our goals whether they are an applicant for employment or an employee requiring special facilitation.

title: Emergency Medicine Fellowship Ambulatory Emergency Care - July 2026
description: Overview: **BEAUMONT HOSPITAL****(Incorporating St Joseph’s Hospital, Raheny)** Beaumont Hospital, incorporating St. Joseph’s Hospital Raheny, is a large academic teaching hospital 5km north of Dublin City centre. Beaumont Hospital provides emergency and acute care services across 54 medical specialties to a local community of some 290,000 people. In addition, Beaumont Hospital is a designated cancer centre and the regional treatment centre for ear, nose and throat, and gastroenterology, and the national referral centre for neurosurgery and neurology, renal transplantation, cochlear implantation and mechanical thrombectomy. Beaumont Hospital is the lead Level 4 hospital within the Royal College of Surgeons Hospital Group, which includes Connolly Hospital, Rotunda Hospital, Cavan/Monaghan Hospitals, Louth/Meath Hospitals, and the Royal College of Surgeons. Beaumont Hospital employs approximately 4,000 staff and has a bed complement of 820 beds. Beaumont Hospital is the principal teaching hospital for the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. Beaumont Hospital also enjoys close links with Dublin City University, especially in the area of nurse training, and with other academic institutions in respect of training and research. St. Joseph's Hospital Raheny, is an acute hospital that provides both medical and surgical in\-patient care, day care, outpatient, outpatient physiotherapy and radiology services. A dedicated Care of the Elderly Rehabilitation Unit and a 100 bedded Raheny Community Nursing Unit have also been developed on the St. Joseph’s campus. **Emergency Medicine Directorate Fellowship \-** **Ambulatory Emergency Care****.** **Duration of the Fellowship:** 12 months \- July 2026 to July 2027 **Location/Clinical Site of the Fellowship**Beaumont Hospital, incorporating Emergency Medicine, Neurosurgery and Neurocritical Care. **Fellowship Aims and Overview:** The overall aim of this fellowship is to provide advanced, structured training in the area of Ambulatory Emergency Care. The Fellow will develop a consultant\-level skillset that enhances the clinical experience and outcomes for patients availing of the current ambulatory emergency care area within the Emergency Department (ED). This Fellowship will compromise of a 12 month attachment to the ED and incorporate the following core elements: * Ambulatory and Urgent Emergency Care – the Fellow will work within the ambulatory emergency care area of the ED providing high level expert care to a large patient volume, ensuring streaming from both the triage and post triage spaces, targeting the delivery of safe, efficient and effective care. * Development of Clinical Care Pathways including ‘Alternative Pathways’– the Fellow will expect to gain experience in and assist in the development of Clinical Care Pathways and Alternative Pathways to ED attendance, and work collaboratively with the community and the Acute Admissions Unit, as well as other relevant stakeholders at Beaumont Hospital, in the development of admission avoidance strategies. * Performance Metric Analysis and Monitoring – the Fellow will contribute to and enhance the capture of relevant performance data for the ambulatory emergency care area, contribute to monthly clinical governance meetings and work closely with the wider EM multidiscliniary group to ensure care standards are continuously met and improved . The Fellow will participate in consultant\-led teaching, lead on relevant teaching on pathways development, attend and contribute to relevant governance meetings, as well as national and international meetings. Supervision will be provided by Consultant’s in Emergency Medicine as well as the wider EM Directorate Team **Candidate Profile and Anticipated Working Week:** The successful applicant will hold (or intend to hold prior to commencement of the Fellowship) a CCST in Emergency Medicine. The successful Fellow will work 30 hours per week on the clinical floor with 9 additional hours dedicated to clinical pathway development, research, clinical audit, quality improvement and teaching. Post Specifications: * Each candidate **must** hold a valid **Irish Medical Council Registration** * MRCEM Primary examination * English Language Competency: All doctors taking up employment who were not registered with the Irish Medical Council in any of the divisions of the Register of Medical Practitioners prior to **1st January 2015**, or who did not complete the entirety of their under\-graduate medical training in the Republic of Ireland, are required to provide evidence of one of the following: * Completion of your Medical degree in English from any of the following countries: Australia, Canada, New Zealand, The United States of America or The United Kingdom. A copy of your Degree Certificate is the required evidence **OR*** English Language Tests Certificate: International English Language Test System Certificate (IELTS – minimum overall band score of 7\.0\) **or** Occupational English Test Certificate (OET – minimum overall grade score of B). IELTS/OET test results must be current and dated within two years of the date submitted to the employer. The Emergency Department (ED) in Beaumont Hospital provides care to 60,000 patients each year. The adult only ED is based in an Academic Teaching Hospital with an available bed base of in excess of 800 beds. The Consultant team have been involved in core and higher specialist training in Emergency Medicine for over two decades. There is protected Consultant delivered teaching each week. The ED has an active research and audit programme and the Consultant team have supported Doctors in the performance of research from the concept phase to international presentation and publication in the peer reviewed literature. The Hospital has a Neuroscience centre, Neurosurgical Unit, Oncology and Radiation Oncology Centre and national ENT centre with a very active Stroke Unit and most specialty services are available on site. We take pride in providing high quality care to our patients and clinical support for our Doctors in training. **Closing date for receipt of applications is 2pm on Friday 3rd April 2026\.** Applicants should note that it will not be possible to inform all applicants individually of the outcome of their applications. **Please note that some of the above posts may be filled by recognised training schemes prior to any hospital interviews.****Beaumont Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and supports a no smoking policy**

description: Overview: **BEAUMONT HOSPITAL****(Incorporating St Joseph’s Hospital, Raheny)** Beaumont Hospital, incorporating St. Joseph’s Hospital Raheny, is a large academic teaching hospital 5km north of Dublin City centre. Beaumont Hospital provides emergency and acute care services across 54 medical specialties to a local community of some 290,000 people. In addition, Beaumont Hospital is a designated cancer centre and the regional treatment centre for ear, nose and throat, and gastroenterology, and the national referral centre for neurosurgery and neurology, renal transplantation, cochlear implantation and mechanical thrombectomy. Beaumont Hospital is the lead Level 4 hospital within the Royal College of Surgeons Hospital Group, which includes Connolly Hospital, Rotunda Hospital, Cavan/Monaghan Hospitals, Louth/Meath Hospitals, and the Royal College of Surgeons. Beaumont Hospital employs approximately 4,000 staff and has a bed complement of 820 beds. Beaumont Hospital is the principal teaching hospital for the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. Beaumont Hospital also enjoys close links with Dublin City University, especially in the area of nurse training, and with other academic institutions in respect of training and research. St. Joseph's Hospital Raheny, is an acute hospital that provides both medical and surgical in\-patient care, day care, outpatient, outpatient physiotherapy and radiology services. A dedicated Care of the Elderly Rehabilitation Unit and a 100 bedded Raheny Community Nursing Unit have also been developed on the St. Joseph’s campus. **Emergency Medicine Directorate Fellowship \-** **Neurotrauma and Neurocritical Care in Emergency Medicine.** **Duration of the Fellowship:** 12 months \- July 2026 to July 2027 **Location/Clinical Site of the Fellowship**Beaumont Hospital, incorporating Emergency Medicine, Neurosurgery and Neurocritical Care. **Fellowship Aims and Overview:** The overall aim of this fellowship is to provide advanced, structured training in the resuscitation and multidisciplinary management of traumatic brain injury (TBI) and neurocritical care. The Fellow will develop a consultant\-level skillset that improves patient outcomes and contribute to the enhancement of neurotrauma and neurocritical care provision in Ireland. This Fellowship will compromise of two 6 months rotations: * Emergency Medicine – 6 months: this component focuses on early and effective neuro\-resuscitation, trauma airway management, ventilation strategies for severe TBI and the optimisation of physiological parameters known to influence secondary brain injury, as well as the standardisation and development of relevant neurotrauma and neurocritical care pathways between the ED, the wider hospital and relevant national stakeholders. * Neurocritical Care and Neurosurgery – 6 months: this attachment will facilitate high level training in the management of severe TBI and neurocritically unwell patients in the Richmond and General ICUs, as well as exposure to both operative and non\-operative management strategies for this patient cohort. The Fellow will gain experience in the interpretation of neuroimaging by attending MDMs and participate in relevant neurosurgical interventions, ward rounds and multidisciplinary meeting The Fellow will participate in consultant\-led teaching, lead on interdisciplinary simulation training in neurotrauma and neuro\-resuscitation, attend and contribute to relevant governance meetings, as well as national and international meetings. Supervision will be provided by Consultant’s in Emergency Medicine supported by a Consultant Neurosurgeon and a Consultant Neuroanaesthesiologist / Intensivist. **Candidate Profile and Anticipated Working Week:** The successful applicant will hold (or intend to hold prior to commencement of the Fellowship) a CCST in Emergency Medicine. The successful Fellow will work 30 hours per week on the clinical floor with 9 additional hours dedicated to clinical pathway development, research, clinical audit, quality improvement and interdisciplinary teaching. Post Specifications: * Each candidate **must** hold a valid **Irish Medical Council Registration** * MRCEM Primary examination * English Language Competency: All doctors taking up employment who were not registered with the Irish Medical Council in any of the divisions of the Register of Medical Practitioners prior to **1st January 2015**, or who did not complete the entirety of their under\-graduate medical training in the Republic of Ireland, are required to provide evidence of one of the following: * Completion of your Medical degree in English from any of the following countries: Australia, Canada, New Zealand, The United States of America or The United Kingdom. A copy of your Degree Certificate is the required evidence **OR*** English Language Tests Certificate: International English Language Test System Certificate (IELTS – minimum overall band score of 7\.0\) **or** Occupational English Test Certificate (OET – minimum overall grade score of B). IELTS/OET test results must be current and dated within two years of the date submitted to the employer. The Emergency Department (ED) in Beaumont Hospital provides care to 60,000 patients each year. The adult only ED is based in an Academic Teaching Hospital with an available bed base of in excess of 800 beds. The Consultant team have been involved in core and higher specialist training in Emergency Medicine for over two decades. There is protected Consultant delivered teaching each week. The ED has an active research and audit programme and the Consultant team have supported Doctors in the performance of research from the concept phase to international presentation and publication in the peer reviewed literature. The Hospital has a Neuroscience centre, Neurosurgical Unit, Oncology and Radiation Oncology Centre and national ENT centre with a very active Stroke Unit and most specialty services are available on site. We take pride in providing high quality care to our patients and clinical support for our Doctors in training. **Closing date for receipt of applications is 2pm on Friday 3rd April 2026\.** Applicants should note that it will not be possible to inform all applicants individually of the outcome of their applications. **Please note that some of the above posts may be filled by recognised training schemes prior to any hospital interviews.****Beaumont Hospital is an equal opportunities employer and supp****orts a no smoking policy**

title: Employer Branding Specialist
description: We are seeking a highly motivated, detail oriented and creative Employer Brand Specialist to join our Recruitment \& Employer Brand Team on a 12\-month fixed term contract. The Employer Brand Specialist will be responsible primarily, but not exclusively, for managing projects and day\-to\-day operational activities within the Recruitment \& Employer Brand team. We are looking for someone who can juggle a wide array of tasks with tight deadlines in a fast\-paced environment. The Employer Brand Specialist will work closely with other departments across and must be able to communicate and collaborate in a positive and professional manner with all parties internally \& externally. What you'll do * Manage and implement recruitment projects, including creation of project plans, engaging with stakeholders, forming and leading the project team * Coordinate our employer presence at careers fairs and university sponsorship events * Liaise with our marketing agency in relation to recruitment marketing activities (e.g. design of recruitment brochures, job announcements, participation in careers fairs, internet presence) to support the creation of a sustainable employer brand * Provide operational support in the development and improvement of targeted marketing around our management development programmes * Provide support to the business on recruitment needs such as Graduate Recruitment * Partner with regional colleagues to ensure all recruitment and recruitment marketing needs are met * Organise assessment centres, screening processes, and interview scheduling * Develop and manage national employer brand ambassadors * Act as the Top Employer handler, supporting all activities related to the certification process and ongoing compliance * Organise and coordinate recruitment video shoots/photoshoots * Coordinate partnerships with external suppliers, such as *T* he Irish Times and Gradireland * Work on sponsored articles and media interviews with The Irish Times, in close collaboration with our Corporate Affairs department What you'll need * Minimum of 1 year experience in Recruitment/Talent Acquisition * 3rd level qualification in HR is beneficial * Full clean driving licence is essential * Excellent communication skills with the ability to form, establish and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholder * Ability to work under pressure as part of a dynamic team * Excellent time management skills, organisational skill and attention to detail * Strong analytical skills to measure performance and understand key performance indicators * Excellent project management skills with an ability to set and achieve goals, meet deadlines and multi\-task What you'll receive * €51,000 pro rata * 20 days holidays * Private employee medical insurance * Company pension * Flexible start and finish times * Initial training and on\-going development from an experienced team member * Excellent opportunities for career progression * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Dynamic work environment * Modern office facility with free parking * On\-site gym and canteen * Hybrid working model \- up to 2 days remote working per week * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Maternity \& Paternity Leave top up, Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme

title: Environmental Technician
description: **Overview** Element has an opportunity for an **Environmental Technician** whose prime responsibility is to assist the regional environmental field services department in environmental testing and reporting at project sites. **Responsibilities** * Assist and learn all aspects of work the environmental department carry out including stack emissions monitoring, ambient air quality monitoring, soil and groundwater monitoring and noise monitoring * A willingness to embrace new skills in the above mentioned areas and apply them in a practical context is considered prerequisite * Assist with planning and execution of projects * Assist with report preparation * Assistance with maintenance and calibration of environmental department equipment * Assistance with maintenance of Health and Safety and Quality standards within a UKAS accredited 17025 quality management systems. **Skills / Qualifications** * Science Graduate or higher qualification * 2 years relevant experience * Physical fitness and ability to withstand the challenging working conditions presented by the climbing of stacks and handling heavy industrial * Strong and determined quality focus with indefatigable determination to get the job done under the most challenging conditions * Self\-motivated team playing with excellent organizational and communication skills * Willingness to embrace new skills and apply them in a practical context is considered prerequisite **Company Overview** Element is one of the fastest growing testing, inspection and certification businesses in the world. Globally we have more than 9,000 brilliant minds operating from 270 sites across 30 countries. Together we share an ambitious purpose to ‘Make tomorrow safer than today’. When failure in use is not an option, we help customers make certain that their products, materials, processes and services are safe, compliant and fit for purpose. From early R\&D, through complex regulatory approvals and into production, our global laboratory network of scientists, engineers, and technologists support customers to achieve assurance over product quality, sustainable outcomes, and market access. While we are proud of our global reach, working at Element feels like being part of a smaller company. We empower you to take charge of your career, and reward excellence and integrity with growth and development. Industries across the world depend on our care, attention to detail and the absolute accuracy of our work. The role we have to play in creating a safer world is much bigger than our organization. **Diversity Statement** At Element, we always take pride in putting our people first. We are an equal opportunity employer that recognizes diversity and inclusion as fundamental to our Vision of becoming “the world’s most trusted testing partner”. All suitably qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment on the basis of objective work related criteria and without regard for the following: age, disability, ethnic origin, gender, marital status, race, religion, responsibility of dependents, sexual orientation, or gender identity or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws.
