title: Senior Fitter / Fitter
description: **We're Hiring: Senior Fitter / Fitter (Maintenance Technician) \- Join a Global Automotive Leader!** ===================================================================================================== ### **Join the DECOTEK Automotive Team \- Driving Precision for Global Automotive Leaders** **DECOTEK Automotive**, based in the Irish midlands, employs a highly skilled workforce in a worldclass manufacturing environment. We design, develop, and manufacture premium exterior trim components for **top global automotive manufacturers** \- and we pride ourselves on supplying original parts directly to some of the biggest names in the industry. We are seeking highly skilled and motivated **Senior Fitters / Fitters (Maintenance Technicians)** to join our dynamic, innovative, and growing manufacturing environment. **This is a fulltime shift role. Candidates must be fully flexible to work a 3cycle shift pattern.** **Why Join DECOTEK Automotive?** -------------------------------- * Work with cutting\-edge manufacturing technology in a highly skilled environment * Be part of a company supplying world\-leading automotive OEMs * Join a culture that values safety, teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement * Grow your career with development, upskilling and crosstraining opportunities **Role Overview** ================= As a Senior Fitter / Fitter at DECOTEK, you'll play a key role in ensuring the optimal performance, reliability and safety of our equipment and production systems. You will support manufacturing, lead problem\-solving at the production cell, and drive continuous improvement for enhanced operational efficiency. **Key Responsibilities** ======================== ### **Maintenance \& Technical Expertise** * Ownership of maintenance and repair for plant machinery, tooling and mechanical systems * Troubleshoot mechanical/electrical/pneumatic/hydraulic issues with minimal downtime * Evaluate, repair and modify tools, mechanical assemblies, and production equipment * Plan and record all maintenance activities via the **PEMAC system** * Perform equipment calibrations, validations, and ensure compliance with CE/safety directives * Service equipment per manufacturer guidelines and technical specifications ### **Production \& Operational Support** * Provide consistent technical support to manufacturing to maximise OEE and output * Attend breakdowns, identify root causes, and action immediate corrective solutions * Lead and support new projects from commissioning to SOP * Participate in shift handovers and daily meetings to ensure seamless communication ### **Continuous Improvement \& Leadership** * Actively drive operational excellence, cost reduction, and efficiency improvements * Identify and resolve process\-related issues using strong diagnostic and analytical skills * Train, mentor and support junior technicians and apprentices * Collaborate crossfunctionally with engineering, production, suppliers and service teams ### **Safety \& Compliance** * Champion safe working practices, risk assessments and adherence to H\&S policies * Maintain all work areas within **6S methodology**: Sort, Set in Order, Shine, Standardise, Sustain \& Safety * Immediately report, escalate or isolate any unsafe equipment, guarding issues, or safety concerns **Skills \& Experience Required** ================================= * Level 6 qualification (or equivalent) in Mechanical Engineering or Maintenance Technology * Experience with complex engineering drawings, mechanical systems and diagnostic equipment * Strong troubleshooting skills with hands\-on experience in mechanical systems; exposure to electrical/pneumatic/hydraulic systems is a strong advantage * Experience with CMMS systems (SAP \& PEMAC preferred) * Excellent communication, organisational and problem\-solving abilities * Ability to work independently and as part of a high\-performing team * Flexibility to support shutdown periods, bank holidays, weekends when required * A strong commitment to personal development, upskilling, and continuous improvement **Ideal Candidates Will:** ========================== * Display leadership and initiative at all times * Embrace challenges and adapt to evolving technologies * Maintain exceptional attention to detail and quality * Thrive in a fast\-paced, technical environment * Want to grow and advance within a leading manufacturing organisation **Ready to Build the Future of Automotive Excellence?** ======================================================= If you're a skilled Fitter or Maintenance Technician looking for your next career step with a forward\-thinking automotive manufacturer, **we want to hear from you**. **Apply today and become part of the DECOTEK Automotive team.**

title: Senior Hydrogeologist
description: **Purpose \& Scope of the Role** Join a well\-established and growing groundwater team within a wider geo\-environmental group of around 100 professionals operating across the UK and Ireland. This role supports the delivery of high\-profile groundwater frameworks and resource development projects, including work with water utilities and public sector clients. As a Consultant or Senior Hydrogeologist, you will focus on technical delivery, project management and the continued growth of groundwater services, contributing to both regional and national projects. **Your Impact in this Position** You will play a key role in developing sustainable groundwater resources, advising on feasibility, investigation, testing and implementation of new water supplies. Your expertise will directly influence abstraction licensing, environmental protection and water resource management. Alongside technical delivery, you will strengthen client relationships, support project delivery and contribute to the ongoing success and expansion of the groundwater business. **Core Capabilities** **Technical Delivery** * Deliver hydrogeological assessments from desk study through to reporting and implementation * Design and interpret pumping tests and groundwater investigations * Prepare abstraction licence applications and technical reports * Apply best practice and UK/NI regulatory guidance to groundwater projects **Project \& Commercial Management** * Manage project budgets, programmes and subcontractors * Support tendering, fee proposals and delivery of projects (£100k\+) * Maintain high standards of health, safety and quality across all work **Business Development \& Client Engagement** * Act as a key client contact, building strong working relationships * Support growth through frameworks and new opportunities * Contribute to client meetings, proposals and project delivery strategies **Fieldwork \& Site Leadership** * Plan and supervise field investigations, drilling and testing * Oversee water quality sampling and environmental assessments * Carry out site visits to support technical delivery and problem\-solving **Collaboration \& Mentoring** * Work within a multi\-disciplinary team across geo\-environmental services * Support and mentor junior team members * Contribute to technical excellence and knowledge sharing **Skills, Qualifications \& Experience** A degree in earth sciences, geology or geochemistry, with postgraduate qualification in hydrogeology. Strong experience in groundwater investigations, pumping test analysis and reporting is essential. You will have excellent communication skills, technical judgement and the ability to manage projects and client relationships. Chartered status and experience with modelling tools (e.g. MODFLOW) are advantageous. **Working Environment** This role can be based in Wexford or Belfast, with flexibility to work from other offices for experienced candidates. Projects span Great Britain, Ireland and occasionally international locations, with short periods of travel required. Hybrid and flexible working arrangements are supported, typically combining office, site and remote working. Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. We support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are *Leading with Science®* to solve our clients’ most complex problems. *In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role* Additional Information * Organization: 632 TIE

title: Senior Planning Manager
description: **Job Description** Reporting to the Operations Manager the planning Manager is responsible for leading project planning activities across major MEP projects in Ireland, Europe \& UK. The successful candidate will be responsible for developing, managing, and overseeing all project programmes, ensuring accurate progress reporting, critical path analysis, and effective communication with stakeholders. **Duties And Responsibilities** * Develop, maintain, and manage detailed project schedules using Primavera P6 and/or MS Project * Create baseline programmes and manage schedule updates * Conduct critical path analysis, resource loading, and forecasting * Identify programme risks and recommend mitigation strategies * Produce weekly and monthly progress reports * Support commercial teams with delay analysis and EoT submissions * Present programme updates to internal and external stakeholders * Collaborate with project, engineering, commercial, and site teams * Lead and mentor junior planners * Implement best‑practice planning processes and improve planning controls **Qualifications** **SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS** * 10\+ years planning experience in construction or MEP environments * Strong expertise in Primavera P6 (essential) * Experience delivering complex, high‑value projects * Strong understanding of project controls and risk management * Ability to interpret engineering drawings and specifications * Excellent communication and stakeholder‑management skills * Proven leadership and team‑management experience * Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or related field * PMI‑SP or similar certification beneficial * Experience in large‑scale MEP projects is desirable Additional Information Having an inclusive and flexible culture helps the business continue to grow as a strong, dynamic and innovative organisation. As Well As Being Supported And Encouraged To Develop Your Career Here At King And Moffatt, We Also Offer The Following * Competitive salary * Professional Development support * Employee Assistance Program * Health and Wellness programs At King and Moffatt, we believe in the power of diversity and inclusion to drive innovation, creativity, and success. We are committed to creating a workplace where all employees feel welcomed, valued, and respected regardless of their race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, disability, or any other dimension of diversity. We strive to build a culture that celebrates differences, encourages open dialogue, and promotes equal opportunities for growth and advancement. By embracing diversity and fostering an inclusive environment, we not only strengthen our organisation but also enrich the lives of our employees and the communities we serve

title: Senior Project Manager - Fit Out
description: **Title:** Senior Project Manager **Location:** Dublin 2 (Hybrid) **Salary:** €75,000 \- €85,000 per annum \+ Bonus \+ Pension \+ Health Care \+ perks **The Company:** Our client is a global commercial real estate organisation. They have a track record as a leader within the commercial property sector in Ireland and globally. They count some of the world's best known software and fintech companies as their clients and offer fantastic career progression. This is an expanding and fast\-paced team, overseeing the delivery of large scale fit\-outs and refurbishment works across a range of sectors. **The Role:** This role will suit a driven professional with strong experience in commercial office fit\-out projects, looking to work across a diverse portfolio of high\-profile assignments. You will play a key role in delivering projects from concept through to completion, working closely with clients, consultants, and internal stakeholders. With a secured pipeline of work, the position provides excellent scope for career development and the opportunity to contribute to the growth of client partnerships while leading and mentoring internal teams. The ideal candidate will demonstrate a high level of accountability, the ability to organise and manage competing priorities to achieve deadlines, and a proactive, results\-driven approach, with proven capability to establish and nurture effective working relationships. **Key Responsibilities:** * Lead and manage multiple commercial fit\-out projects across all phases, from concept design through to construction and handover * Develop and manage detailed project programmes, budgets, and reporting structures to ensure successful delivery * Chair project meetings, prepare agendas, and provide regular progress updates to stakeholders * Coordinate design teams, consultants, and contractors to ensure alignment with project objectives * Oversee procurement processes, including selection and appointment of consultants and construction teams * Review and manage project financials, including requisitions, change orders, and invoicing * Provide strategic advice to clients and internal stakeholders on project delivery and risk management * Prepare and present project reports to senior stakeholders, ensuring transparency and accountability * Collaborate with property and facility management teams to align project delivery with operational requirements * Support business development activities, including client presentations and service proposals * Mentor and support junior project team members where required * Monitor performance, quality, and output across project teams * Ensure compliance with all relevant regulations, standards, and governance requirements * Provide clear recommendations and reporting on project risks, challenges, and outcomes * Ensure all approvals and documentation are secured prior to financial commitments or project changes **Key Requirements:** * 5\+ years’ experience in project management, ideally within commercial office fit\-out * Strong technical understanding of construction, design, and project delivery processes * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills * Proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously * Strong commercial awareness and financial management capability * Leadership experience or exposure to managing teams is advantageous

title: Senior Quantity Surveyor
description: **About the role** The Senior Quantity Surveyor will take a lead role in delivering Quantity Surveying services, managing client relationships, and supporting the development of ORS’s Quantity Surveying capability. This role suits a commercially astute Quantity Surveyor who enjoys autonomy and influence within a growing team. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead commercial management of projects from inception to completion * Preparation and review of cost plans, cost reports, and cashflows * Oversight of procurement strategy and tender evaluation * Management of subcontractor accounts, variations, and final accounts * Identification and management of commercial risks and opportunities * Client\-facing role, providing commercial advice and reporting * Mentoring and support of junior QS staff (as the team grows) * Contribution to the development of ORS QS processes and best practice **Essential Requirements** * Degree\-qualified in Quantity Surveying or related discipline * 6\+ years’ relevant Quantity Surveying experience * Chartered (MRICS) or working toward chartership desirable * Strong knowledge of standard forms of contract * Proven experience managing projects independently * Excellent stakeholder management and negotiation skills * Irish project experience essential **What We Can Offer You** Being part of ORS means more than just a job, it means joining a company that invests in you and your ambitions with transparency, structure and support. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join ORS, which include: * Competitive salary * Performance\-related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Remote\-first hybrid model – use your home as a base and any of our office locations as you see fit * Work from abroad * Flexible working hours * Access to over 60 health disciplines through our Kara Connect Platform * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity, paternity and fertility leave * Company and birthday annual leave days * Death in Service benefit * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture, social, wellness and CPD committees * Talent Development Programmes * Access to leading career coach Gerry Duffy **About ORS** ORS is a world\-class multidisciplinary building consultancy with more than 30 years of delivering technical excellence across the built environment. Our mission is simple but powerful: We endeavour to make the lives of our team, clients, partners and communities happier and better in the ways that really matter every single day. Don’t just take our word for it! ORS has won numerous awards, recognising our Great Place to Work culture. * Winner of the Engineering Consultancy of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2025 * Winner of the Graduate Programme of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2025 * Winner of the Best Company to Work For at the Irish Design \& Build Awards 2024 \& 2025 * Winner of the Best People Development of the Year at the Engineering Excellence Awards 2024 * Winner of the Recruitment \& Retention Strategy at the Workplace Excellence Awards 2024 * Great Place to Work certified consecutively since 2017 * Top 3 Company Best Workplaces in the medium\-sized category at the Great Place to Work awards 2025 * Best Workplaces for Health \& Wellbeing 2025 Great Place to Work * Engineers Ireland CPD Accreditation since 20217 * Awarded for Hybrid Way of Working at the 2022 Great Place to Work Awards * Silver Accreditation through Investors in Diversity 2025 ORS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive company culture. We foster a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects and supports their individual strengths and experiences.

title: Senior Service & Support Engineer
description: **Overview:** The Senior Service \& Support Engineer plays a key role in delivering high\-quality technical support for AIR’s automation solutions. Sitting between the Support Engineer and Support Lead Engineer roles, this position combines strong technical capability with enhanced ownership of service processes, mentoring responsibilities, and cross\-functional collaboration. The ideal candidate will demonstrate deep technical expertise, strong problem\-solving skills, and the ability to drive improvements across support operations while working closely with internal SMEs and engineering teams. **Key Responsibilities:** **Service Operations \& Process Ownership** * Support the refinement of service and support processes, identifying operational gaps and recommending improvements. * Assist in coordinating support resources and activities to meet service demand. * Contribute to the development and implementation of Support Service Agreements (SSA). * Proactively identify opportunities for post\-sales business growth (spare parts, upgrades, reworks, training). **Advanced Technical Support** * Provide senior\-level troubleshooting across software, electrical, and mechanical systems. * Act as an escalation point for Support Engineers, assisting with complex or recurring issues. * Perform detailed root\-cause analysis and propose long\-term corrective actions. * Utilise advanced diagnostic tools and methods to resolve issues efficiently. **Customer \& Internal Stakeholder Interaction** * Engage directly with customers to resolve technical issues and provide clear communication of findings. * Translate complex concepts into accessible explanations for non\-technical stakeholders. * Contribute to high levels of customer satisfaction by ensuring timely and effective resolution of issues. **Collaboration \& Mentoring** * Work closely with internal SMEs (software, electrical, mechanical) to address escalated issues. * Share knowledge, best practices, and lessons learned within the support team. * Mentor junior engineers and technicians to build support capability. **Documentation \& Improvement Initiatives** * Develop and maintain technical documentation including troubleshooting guides, SOPs, and internal knowledge base entries. * Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives across service tools, processes, and customer workflows. * Keep current with industry trends and emerging technologies. **Qualifications \& Experience:** · L6 Craft qualification in Electrical/Mechanical or bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or related field. · Significant experience in technical support roles with exposure to automation systems. · Strong cross\-disciplinary understanding of software, electrical, and mechanical systems. · Experience supporting team members or leading small workstreams is an advantage. **Key Skills \& Attributes** * Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. * Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively across functions. * Customer\-focused mindset with high standards of service delivery. * Proactive, organised, and improvement oriented. **What we offer:** In AIR, we offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees' well\-being and professional development, including company bonus, pension plan, training support, and various wellness initiatives. **About AIR:** AIR is a modern, global manufacturing automation company, newly formed but built on decades of experience from its established business units. We combine deep industry expertise with a dynamic, forward\-looking approach to deliver innovative, reliable automation solutions. We operate across five key sites located in the USA, UK, and Ireland, serving customers worldwide with a unified commitment to quality, speed, and technical excellence. Today, AIR operates as a fully integrated global organization, partnering with customers to help bring their products to life and scale their operations. Our mission is to empower innovation, scalability, and long\-term support by combining the engineering excellence of our people with the operational capabilities of our global facilities. This job description is intended as a summary of the primary responsibilities and qualifications for this position. It is not exhaustive and may be subject to change. AIR is an Equal Opportunities Employer. If you are living in Ireland and hold a valid work permit, we would love to hear from you, if however, you do not hold a valid work permit unfortunately we will not be able to assist you with your job search. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€65,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Senior Sonographer & Trainee Obstetrics Sonographer | Dún Laoghaire
description: **Job Description** **Job role:** Medica Group is the market leader in the provision of teleradiology and managed radiology services to the UK and Ireland. We are pleased to offer a unique development opportunity for a CORU\-registered Sonographer who wishes to expand their practice into obstetrics and early pregnancy ultrasound while maintaining and developing their general ultrasound skills. As we are constantly expanding our business, at this moment, we require specialisations in MSK, DDH or paediatrics. However, if you do not possess any of them but have extensive hands\-on experience in general ultrasound, please get in touch! title: Sr. Director, Analyst - AI/Agentic Systems in Digital Workplace Applications For MS Copilot (Remote Europe)
description: Gartner Analysts are industry thought leaders who create must\-have insights, market predictions and best practices for a broad range of world\-leading organizations. requests that accelerate the client's ability to act * Develop in\-depth analysis to identify the root cause of a client’s barriers or overall needs and reframe thinking to drive strategy forward * processes that help the team and BTI provide excellent service delivery * Be a mentor and a coach by supporting more junior team members * Be client\-centric while actively seeking to help clients engage regularly and often with Gartner insights and interactions **What you will need:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; Graduate degree preferred * 12\+ years of relevant field or Gemini Enterprise and Vertex AI; Open AI’s ChatGPT Enterprise and Agent Kit) * Digital workplace and end\-user computing modernization, with a specific focus on the integration of AI * Managing AI systems architecture, implementation, integration, and orchestration processes * Evaluating and/or embedding AI governance and risk controls across diverse business units to ensure secure, enterprise\-wide adoption * Demonstrate executive presence; can immediately establish credibility with executives and additional stakeholders * Strong organizational skills; ability to work under tight deadlines and produce high quality deliverables * Demonstrate excellence in research and writing ability * Strong written and verbal proficiency, analytical and presentation skills; ability to engage clients and respond effectively to questions * Proficient in analyzing and synthesizing data; can effectively apply patterns and frameworks while drawing and defending conclusions to client challenges * Strong communicator who is able to explain complex concepts concisely and simply * Subject matter expert comfortable presenting at large and small\-scale speaking engagements * Strong business and financial acumen * Deep knowledge of the global and competitive landscape within subject area as well as the interplay in that market * Ability to work independently, while also being intrinsically motivated to collaborate across teams and support the workflow of others, in a multicultural global team * Learning agile and adept with navigating highly matrixed environments * Ability to represent Gartner's research methodology and strategies effectively at all levels * Willingness and ability to travel up to 25% (where applicable) Remote **Who are we?** At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world. Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission\-critical priorities. Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 21,000 associates globally who support \~14,000 client enterprises in \~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here. **What makes Gartner a great place to work?** Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance. We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients. Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations. We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work. **What do we offer?** Gartner offers world\-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers. In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring. Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us. title: Staff Technical Program Manager
description: The Core Platforms team is a diverse group of individuals across Europe and India, responsible for building MongoDB’s Enterprise Advanced product, helping our customers to run MongoDB on any type of infrastructure at global scale. Our software and services allow users to deploy fault\-tolerant, globally distributed MongoDB clusters in minutes. The main focus of this department is to adapt our software to manage MongoDB clusters which are deployed in data centres or private and hybrid cloud platforms. As a Staff Technical Program Manager, you will own the successful delivery of complex, cross\-functional efforts. You will partner closely with stakeholders across the organization from Engineering, Product, Product Design, and Field\-facing teams to develop and execute against our overall product vision and business objectives, uncover solutions to technical challenges, and provide data\-driven insights that power strategic decision\-making. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. Position Expectations * Leverage technical acumen and analytical skills to drive engineering programs forward and maximize business value delivery * Recognise patterns in a sea of information and take action accordingly * Develop a strategic approach to program management that balances agility and process. This involves discerning when to provide direct guidance and when to empower teams by establishing clear frameworks and guidelines for ongoing initiatives * Build strategic partnerships with Product, Design, and Engineering stakeholders * Expand knowledge into new domains as called upon * Act as a mentor to junior TPMs and lead by example * Embody MongoDB's core values and leadership principles The right candidate for this role will be * Experienced with 10\+ years of working in an engineering organization leading complex cross\-functional technical programs * Experienced with 5\+ years of Software development background * Able to partner with technical stakeholders in methodically breaking down engineering challenges to deliver against abstract goals * Able to participate in technical design discussions and summarize trade\-offs, outcomes, and next steps * Skilled at communicating across a diverse set of engineers and stakeholders * Capable of deep user empathy and adept at balancing product goals and engineering constraints to deliver impactful experiences * Hyper\-organized and capable of coordinating across multiple independent work streams and organizations * A role model for effective execution practices, driven by an attuned sense of priority and urgency * Able to leverage their experience in program delivery to influence improvements to our tools, operations, architecture * Trained in working with project tracking software (e.g. Jira, Rally, MS Project) * Familiar with MongoDB or a comparable technology Nice to have * Exposed to generative AI and Machine Learning * Interested in business automation work such as scripting in Python, Google Apps Script and Slack * Familiar with tools and methodologies for code analysis, refactoring, and automated testing Success Measures * Delivery against program goals * Improvements and automations in team operations, including leveraging best practices from the TPM community * Drive department\-wide change and continuous improvement **About MongoDB** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software. Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB. To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. Req ID: 1273394181

title: Talent Acquisition Co-ordinator
description: Vacancy Number: 28204 Category: Human Resources Job Type: Full\-time **Position Description** We are looking to recruit a Talent Acquisition Coordinator to meet the growing demands of the business within the Early Careers Talent Acquisition team. The successful applicant will work in the Talent Acquisition Centre of Excellence “CoE” and will be provided with the support and development needed to enable them to deliver successfully in the role. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage and monitor the advertising of resources in line with the Strategic Resource Plan and approval systems, working closely with HR Governance Specialists. * Coordinate and manage all candidate and applicant communications, interview scheduling, and support line managers and candidates throughout internal and external recruitment processes. * Maintain and update internal trackers and collate data to produce regular management, TA, and HR reports, including monthly recruitment metrics and KPIs. * Assist with Early Talent recruitment, including attending careers fairs, supporting graduate event coordination, and ensuring excellent relationships with all stakeholders. * Review and monitor employment contracts and documentation for external candidates and oversee the new hire process alongside the Onboarding Specialist to ensure compliance with legal and policy requirements. * Maintain the TA SharePoint site, respond to GDPR queries, action data clearance requests, and ensure ongoing compliance with data protection regulations. * Champion continuous improvement initiatives with TA Team Leaders, stay updated on recruitment market trends and employment legislation, and demonstrate flexibility and adaptability to change. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * At least 1\-2 years of relevant work experience. * Ability to meet tight deadlines with accuracy and attention to detail. * Knowledge of HR practices, policies, procedures, and employment legislation. * Proficiency in MS Office, especially advanced Excel, and familiarity with SharePoint. * Strong communication, interpersonal, planning, and organisational skills. * Flexible approach to working. * Ability to work effectively within a team. **Desirable** * A HR qualification or equivalent relevant work experience would be an advantage. * SharePoint and SAP knowledge. **Location** This position will be based in 27 Fitzwilliam Street Lower, Dublin 2\. *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** Early Careers Team Lead **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €33,500\- €40,000 per annum depending on experience **Closing Date** 2nd April 2026 *Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role.* **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Tax Compliance Advisor
description: **Tax Compliance Advisor** **Location:** Ashbourne, Co. Meath **Full\-time, permanent \|** Hybrid (flexible hours available) **The Company** A well\-established accountancy practice is seeking a Tax Compliance Advisor to manage income tax compliance and support clients with tax planning and advisory services. The role involves overseeing tax returns, liaising with clients and revenue authorities, and assisting junior team members, all within a busy, client\-focused practice environment. **Responsibilities** * Manage the income tax function, including client liaison and oversight of tax return preparation * Ensure timely filing and payment of all income tax returns * Review financial records and prior returns to support effective tax planning * Advise clients on tax obligations and identify practical solutions to tax\-related issues * Manage correspondence with clients and tax authorities * Gather and review relevant financial information, including expenses and income details * Supervise and support trainees, ensuring accuracy and compliance * Communicate tax updates and legislative changes to the wider team * Build and maintain strong client relationships * Support the Tax Department with additional duties as required **Requirements** * Minimum 3\+ years' experience in tax or general accountancy practice * Strong knowledge of tax legislation and compliance requirements * Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills * Experience supervising or mentoring junior staff * Proficient in Sage TaxPro, ROS, and Microsoft Office * Highly organised with strong attention to detail and ability to work independently **The Offer** * €50,000 \- €55,000 DOE * Flexible working hours (early finish on Fridays) * Opportunity to join an established and supportive practice * Exposure to a broad and varied client base

title: Tax work placement 2026
description: **Join Crowe Ireland’s 2026 Tax Graduate Programme!** Are you ready to kickstart your career and write your story with one of Ireland's top accounting and advisory firms? At **Crowe Ireland** , we’re not just about numbers we’re about people, innovation, and making a real impact. We’re advisors, partners, and innovators who help businesses grow, navigate their challenges, and build a brighter future. We work with a diverse mix of clients – from owner\-managed business and not\-for \- profit to multinationals, government agencies and hospitality as a small example – Every day brings something new! As part of our team, you'll learn from the best, gain hands \- on experience across a range of industries, and be part of a community that values **collaboration** , **curiosity** , and **creativity** . **Why choose Crowe Ireland?** At Crowe Ireland, we are a close knit team with a global reach. Our firm is all about fostering talent, encouraging fresh ideas, and driving positive change for our clients and community. We are big enough to offer great opportunities, international assignments and write a successful story and small enough for you to make great impacts on some of our projects from the word go. Join us, and you will be part of a dynamic and diverse team that values growth, learning and having a bit of fun along the way! If you want to know more about our Graduate Programme, visit our website . **What’s in it for You?** Our 2026 Tax Graduate Programme is designed to give you real world experience, professional development, and a fast track to becoming a qualified accountant and/or tax advisor. You’ll get to work with amazing clients, use trending technology, get a flavour of all specialties in Tax and learn from a team that genuinely cares about your success. Here’s what you’ll do: **Your Role:** * **Explore a Range of Tax Disciplines** : Get hands on experience with different areas of tax, including International Tax, Corporate Tax, Global Mobility, Personal Taxation and VAT. You’ll help businesses navigate the complexities of tax laws both locally and globally. * **Build leadership skills** : You will be assigned a portfolio of clients to manage. Managers and Partners will coach and support you, also review the quality of the service provided, but you will have the opportunity to build autonomy and leadership skills from day 1\. * **Become a Data Detective** : Use the latest tools and techniques to analyse client data, research tax technical issues, and find smart solutions that add real value to our clients * **Build Meaningful Relationships** : You will work closely with clients and collaborate with other departments to deliver comprehensive solutions that meet their needs. * **Communication Pro** : Ensure managers, directors, and partners are always informed of progress, potential issues, and key insights on your assignments. You will work closely with partners, directors and other managers to deliver top\-notch services to our clients. * **Deliver Excellence** : Contribute to high quality tax assignments, ensuring compliance, mitigating risks, and driving positive outcomes for our clients. **How we are going to support your development:** * Crowe will fully sponsor your accounting qualification: You will have the freedom to choose which accounting qualification you will pursue. ACA? ACCA? It’s your pick! * In addition to your accounting qualification, we will also sponsor a Chartered Tax Advisor (CTA) qualification for you. * We will support your studies by providing generous study leave so you can dedicate time to pass your exams. * Flexibility to attend your classes and tuitions. * You will work closely with our senior team and will be mentored and supported to achieve your personal and professional goals. You will gain experience and develop competencies in practice dealing with clients and carrying out meaningful and relevant work * **What We’re Looking For:** * **Academic Performance** : You achieved or are on track to achieve a 2\.2 or higher in your undergraduate degree and have recently graduated or will graduate in 2026\. * **Future Accountants:** You’re excited about a career in Tax and accounting, no matter your background. * **Eligibility:** You have the right to work in the Republic of Ireland. * **Communication Champs:** You’ve got strong verbal and written communication skills, with an eye for detail and a knack for problem\-solving. * **Attention to Detail:** You’re a master of the small stuff and know that details matter. **Our offer:** * Alongside a fully sponsored accounting qualification, we offer a competitive reward package that comprises salary, benefits, development opportunities and wellbeing programmes. Some of them are: * 22 days of annual leave * Pension with 100% employer matching contributions * Tax saver travel pass * Cycle to Work Scheme * And more… * Work life balance: our team enjoy compressed working weeks during the summer and hybrid work options. Accelerate career progression through ongoing support and investment in your development. * **Why Join Us?** Because we believe in nurturing talent from day one. You will get mentorship from experienced professionals, access to ongoing learning and development opportunities, and the chance to make a real impact in a supportive, inclusive environment. Plus, we know how to have fun—whether it is team social events, community events, or just celebrating a job well done! Ready to write a story that matters? Apply now and start your journey with Crowe! **Equal opportunity** Crowe is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and anyone seeking employment at Crowe is considered based on merit, qualifications, competence and talent. We don’t regard colour, religion, race, national origin, sexual orientation, ancestry, citizenship, sex, marital or family status, disability, gender, or any other legally protected status when making our hiring decisions. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

title: Test Automation Engineer
description: **Test Automation Engineer** ---------------------------- Do you want to join an exciting, growing team that wants to hear your ideas? A team that wants you to be up to date on the latest technologies. Working in BearingPoint you will be part of a team that is passionate about building innovative, high\-quality software for its clients. You will be working in agile teams with all the support you need. BearingPoint is growing quickly, and we are currently hiring junior automation testers to join our QA team. **What skills and experience should you have?** ----------------------------------------------- * Passionate about technology, testing and delivering top quality software products. * At least 5 years' experience in test automation. * Practical experience of test automation including tooling selection and test framework design. * Hands\-on technical experience of building both API and UI test automation frameworks in a range of languages and technologies such as jUnit, Selenium, Serenity BDD (Cucumber, Rest Assured) Robot Framework and JMeter. * Implementation, maintenance and execution of automated test suites to ensure thorough testing of requirements in a fast\-paced Agile environment with an emphasis on BDD and BDD automated tools. * Disciplined reporting, tracking, and resolution of product defects. Implementation of reporting technologies, integrated with automated test runs. * Implementation and management of automated test scripts in CI/CD pipelines. * Experience carrying out code reviews and adhering to best practice guidelines. * Clear understanding of defect management lifecycle. * Hands on knowledge of SQL. * Knowledge of scripting languages (Shell, Perl, Python etc.). * Excellent verbal and written communication skills who excels when working in a multi\-disciplinary agile team. **Why you will enjoy working at BearingPoint Ireland?** ------------------------------------------------------- We work in an open and collaborative way, where new ideas are always welcome. BearingPoint is an environment where potential and high performance is recognised and rewarded. **What we offer:** ------------------ You will receive a competitive salary where you are rewarded for both your individual performance and company performance. You will also receive a best\-in\-class benefits package including holidays, private health insurance, pension contributions, gym/club subscription, wellness programmes, mobile phone and much more. In addition, we offer: **Hybrid working –** where each team has autonomy to determine their in\-person and remote working schedule. **Continuous learning –** we understand that it is imperative to invest in our people's development and we provide external training at prestigious universities such as Oxford and Yale **Social events –** our people perform best when they have a good work life balance, monthly events for all to attend **Diversity** – we are committed to having a diverse workforce where each individual feels they belong and can thrive in a work environment with a strong sense of community **Mentoring \& coaching –** every employee has a development manager to help them grow their career with feedback via continuous conversations **Giving Back –** you will have an opportunity to join our many giving back initiatives including our commitment to sustainability. Our Emissions Calculator gathers accurate emissions data and enables our clients to understand their footprint and track progress on emission reduction and pave the way for a better future. **Together, we are more than business.** ---------------------------------------- To see more from our people with their experiences of BearingPoint please visit our careers website www.bearingpoint.com **About BearingPoint** ---------------------- BearingPoint is an independent management and technology consultancy with European roots and a global reach. The company operates in four units: Consulting, Solutions, Business Services, and Ventures. Consulting covers the advisory business; Solutions provides the tools for successful digital transformation, advanced analytics and regulatory requirements; Business Services provides managed services beyond SaaS; Ventures drives the financing and development of start\-ups. BearingPoint's clients include many of the world's leading companies and organizations. The firm has a global consulting network with more than 10,000 people and supports clients in over 75 countries, engaging with them to achieve measurable and sustainable success. BearingPoint is an equal opportunities employer. Please click here to view our privacy policy. **NO AGENCIES** BearingPoint does not accept unsolicited cvs from 3rd party recruitment agencies without a written agreement in place for placements. BearingPoint is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited cvs and no fee will be paid should the candidate be hired by BearingPoint.

title: Trainee Merchandiser
description: Because your perspective shapes trends. Innovate, your way. **Merchandising at Primark** Our merchandising team turn strategic goals into vast opportunities. Here, your insights will maximise our growth as a business and ensure we continue to stay at the forefront in the world of retail fashion. **What You’ll Do As a Trainee Merchandiser** As a Trainee Merchandiser at Primark, you’ll assist the Merchandiser and Assistant Merchandiser in the delivery of our sales, stocks and contribution budgets. You’ll learn about the industry and be given all the tools you need to progress in a Merchandiser role. Primark is a growing business in a competitive industry, with exciting expansion across the UK, Europe, and the US. Your contribution will focus on delivering budgets and line projections, investigating data inaccuracies and forecasting sales. You’ll also have exciting responsibilities relating to products and trends. **What You'll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. * Flexible Working, opportunity for an early Friday finish, and a subsidised cafeteria. * Primark Perks – Discounts with some local partner business to our offices **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Analytical \- An analytical thinker with an eye for detail and numbers, who also loves working in a team. * Curious \- Investigate and resolve data inaccuracies, report on stock overages and shortages and ensure that current commitment records have been maintained accurately. * Customer focused \- Develop a strong knowledge of our global customer base and use this knowledge to offer outstanding value to our customer, and key recommendations to your team. * Strategically minded \- Act as a self\-starter, working on a range of products trading in multiple regions. As your knowledge of your product and customer grows, you’ll identify opportunities – learning from sales reactions. * Never miss an opportunity \- Use your initiative to identify current and future trading opportunities and propose your recommendations to your team. * Total organisation \- Use your passion for people to build key relationships that will enable you to progress deliveries and shipping documentation, drive stakeholder engagement, and collaborate with your team. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. title: Trainee Optical Assistant
description: Are you a caring individual looking to start your career as an Optical Assistant? You’ve come to the right place. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in Swords, our store is a platinum employer and Great Place to Work accredited! **What’s on offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * Full time \- 40 hours \- weekend working and late nights is essential * Salary \- €34,000 OTE * Great career progression opportunities in a friendly store **What we’re looking for?** Some of the responsibilities of this role include: * Welcoming customers into store * Booking in eye tests * Pre\-screening patients * Providing style advice * Measuring frames * Taking phone call **Find out more** If you are comfortable with the responsibilities we’re looking for and are excited by this opportunity, we’re excited to hear from you. Got what we’re looking for? This job will close when it's filled, so don't delay, apply today!

title: Treasury & Payments Manager
description: The Panel has been retained by an Irish plc to manage the search towards the appointment of a **Treasury \& Payments Manager** on a 14\-month maternity cover contract. You will manage a small team and report into the Head of Finance. **The Job:** This role is responsible for the day\-to\-day management of the company's Payments and Accounts Payable (AP) function, along with supporting the delivery of improvements across payments processes, banking and systems. The role will work closely with the Head of Finance to support improvements to the payments and banking environment, including process enhancements, bank rationalisation and system integration. This is a hands\-on operational role with responsibility for execution, coordination and delivery. **Key Responsibilities:** * Payments Operations * Accounts Payable Management * Payments Process Improvement * Banking and Payments Administration * Systems and Integration Support * Governance and Controls **Required Experience:** * Graduate in business or related discipline * Professional qualification advantageous (ACCA, ACA, ACT, etc) * Minimum 5 years’ working within treasury/payments * Good payments and treasury systems experience * Strong knowledge of bank account management, bank payments, etc * Positive disposition and curious **Remuneration** This exciting opportunity come with a competitive base salary, completion bonus and extensive benefits package.

title: Trust & Safety New Associate - GTNS - Viacon - Persian CL13
description: **Job Title:** Trust \& Safety New Associate. **What are we looking for:** Primary skill \- Content Assessment \- P4 Secondary skill \- Content Moderation \- P4 . **Designation:** Trust \& Safety New Associate. **s and Responsibilities:** In this you are required to solve routine problems, largely through precedent and referral to general guidelines. Your primary interaction is within your own team and your direct supervisor. In this you will be given detailed instructions on all tasks. The decisions that you make impact your own work and are closely supervised. You will be an individual contributor as a part of a team with a predetermined, narrow scope of work. Please note that this may require you to work in rotational shifts. **Location:** Dublin. Skill Required User Generated Content Moderation Content Assessment P4 . Qualification High School Diploma GED . YOE 0 to 1 years. Lang Persian C1 . What would you do You should have an interest in protecting and improving the user experience of consumer products and services. You should be willing to develop a critical understanding of the abuse and policy issues and be comfortable reviewing a large volume of videos to help us keep our site policy compliant. Review, classify or remove content according to client guidelines, using specific tools and channels. Understand and remain updated on changing client policies and guidelines. Participate in process improvement initiatives that improve quality and efficiency of work. Participate in continuous training programs and work group discussions for optimal development in the role. A process to discover what content already exists and how content is planned, created, approved, produced, and maintained. Includes the tasks by which insights and business customer requirements are driven from research, competition reviews, stakeholder interviews and analysis of customer engagement drivers to identify gaps and offer differentiating and innovative solutions..

title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (SURGERY)
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (SURGERY)** **Department: UCD Beacon Hospital Academy \& UCD School of Medicine** **Reports to:** **Administrative \& Clinical Tutor Responsibilities:** Dr Ruth Pilkington, Director of UCD Beacon Academy, Beacon Hospital **Date: July 2026 (Changeover)** **Available as Full\-time (Tutor \+ Clinical) or Part\-time (Tutor only)** **Overall Purpose of Job** Beacon Hospital has an academic partnership with University College Dublin and one of our principle objectives within this partnership is to support the clinical education programme in the UCD School of Medicine. The clinical tutor role plays an instrumental role in identifying key teaching moments for our undergraduate students who are attached to specialities as part of their experiential learning. All clinical tutors are part of a vibrant community of academic staff who pass on their clinical experience and expertise to the next generation of medical professionals. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables: Clinical Tutor (Surgery)** 1\.1\. Coordinate and participate in the teaching and training of the students in Beacon Hospital. 1\.1\.1\. Provide tutorial support and facilitate shared learning experiences for students while on placements. 1\.1\.2\. Provide lectures as part of the formal clinical curriculum and assist with the preparation of clinical examinations. 1\.1\.3\. Provide a level of supervision of clinical activity appropriate to the competence and experience of the individual student. 1\.1\.4\. Utilise a range of appropriate methods of teaching and assessment to demonstrate clinical learning opportunities \- i.e. OSCE style tutorials, problem\-based learning and bed\-side tutorials. 1\.2\. Liaise with relevant UCD personnel to ensure that clinical placements satisfy academic requirements and ensure integrated delivery of all courses. 1\.3\. Coordinate and partake in the orientation and appraisal of medical students attending Beacon Hospital. 1\.4\. Partake in the competency assessment of interns using the national intern competency framework tools within 30 working days of the intern's commencement date. 1\.5\. Demonstrate leadership by assisting in the professional and personal development of the students and Interns throughout their clinical placement at the Beacon Hospital through promotion of self\-learning, formal lectures and tutorials. 1\.6\. Act as a resource for students and Interns seeking specialty information and guidance. 1\.7\. Ensure that the relevant information about progress and performance is made available to the Beacon Hospital and UCD student placement coordinator. 1\.8\. Act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of the UCD Beacon Hospital Academy. 1\.9\. Adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI standards, Code Blue, Infection Prevention and Control, Falls Reduction and data protection. 1\.10\. Contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall clinical education is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. 1\.11\. Build upon the library of tutorial content available in the hospital. The tutor is required to prepare tutorials that can be used in each of the module s \- i.e. Surgery A/B and Clin Res 1, and that can be reproduced with ease each year by clinical tutors at the Beacon Hospital. These may include high level overviews of certain topics that can be taught to students with supporting artefacts, media, and learning tools. 1\.12\. Engage actively in research and scholarly activity as appropriate to the school or hospital. 1\.13\. Champion the student and Interns' participation in clinical audits to ensure that UCD Beacon Hospital Academy programmes meet quality and patient safety standards and any other applicable performance measures. 1\.14\. Report to the appointed clinical lead consultant within the Beacon in all matters relating to medical students and Interns in the manner requested by said clinical lead. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Current relevant Full Irish Medical Council Registration. MRCS/Membership of relevant college is desirable **Experience** Minimum 3 years postgraduate experience in an acute hospital setting. Experience in clinical education of students and junior staff. Experience in the effective management of a poor performing staff member and or student. Attendance at clinical tutor trainee days is required (if not complete the successful candidate will be given the opportunity to attend.) **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** High\-level interpersonal and communication skills (both oral and written) facilitating effective interaction within the work areas, the university and other institutions is a mandatory requirement. Strong organisational skills, ability to prioritise work, to work to deadlines and to use own initiative is a mandatory requirement. Proven experience of working in a quality assurance/improvement environment is a mandatory requirement. Proven ability to work as part of a team is required. Ability to embrace change in a dynamic environment, willingness to adopt new procedures and undertake further appropriate training as required Experience in teaching and examining at third level is desirable. Experience in curriculum development and the development of clinical education programmes is desirable. Evidence of research and peer reviewed publications is desirable. Good knowledge of university rules, regulations and procedures, especially those pertaining to examinations, registration and student welfare is required. Experience in research development at third level is desirable. Experience of general and academic administration is desirable. Display awareness and appreciation of the service users and the ability to empathise with and treat others with dignity and respect. Demonstrate sufficient clinical knowledge and evidence\-based practice to carry the out duties and responsibilities of the role. Demonstrate an ability to apply knowledge to best practice standards. All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: UKI Senior Paid Search Executive
description: ### **UKI****Senior Paid Search Executive** We’re looking for a Senior Paid Search Executive to join our Search team at Flutter UK \& Ireland. This role plays a critical part in driving high quality acquisition at scale across the UK \& Ireland through best\-in\-class paid search execution. You’ll take senior ownership of performance across Google Ads, Microsoft/Bing, and Apple Search Ads, helping to shape strategy, lead optimisation, and deliver sustained commercial growth across some of Flutter’s most recognisable brands. Success in this role will be measured through your ability to scale profitable volume, improve CPA and LTV, and continuously evolve our paid search approach through testing, automation and innovation. As a Senior Paid Search Executive, you’ll be a core member of the central UK \& Ireland Search team, working across a broad portfolio of market\-leading Flutter brands including Betfair, Paddy Power and Sky Betting \& Gaming. You’ll combine hands\-on campaign ownership with a more strategic lens, supporting not just execution but also the evolution of our approach to paid search at scale. You’ll play a key role in shaping best practice across the channel, mentoring junior team members, and working closely with Paid Search Managers and cross\-functional stakeholders to ensure our activity is delivering against clear commercial and customer outcomes. The role sits at the intersection of performance, automation, creative testing and commercial strategy, with accountability across always\-on acquisition, trading moments, and new product or market launches. This is a high impact role offering the opportunity to influence major budgets in a fast\-paced, performance\-driven environment. ### **What you’ll do** * Lead the day\-to\-day management, optimisation and scaling of Paid Search activity across Google Ads, Microsoft/Bing and Apple Search Ads * Take ownership of performance against CPA, volume, LTV and wider commercial targets * Shape bid strategies, budget allocation, account structure and automation frameworks * Drive continuous testing across keywords, creative formats and platform innovation * Provide hands\-on leadership and guidance to more junior team members within the Search team * Work closely with Senior Search Managers to plan channel strategy aligned to UK \& Ireland business priorities * Partner with brand, product, analytics and commercial teams to unlock performance opportunities * Deliver clear insight, performance narratives and recommendations to senior stakeholders * Support major trading moments, regulatory changes and strategic growth initiatives across the region ### **How you’ll do it** Strong proven experience managing Paid Search at scale across Google Ads and Microsoft/Bing (Apple Search Ads experience is highly desirable) * Deep understanding of performance marketing principles including CPA, ROAS, LTV, incrementality and attribution * Experience managing seven\-figure budgets in fast\-paced, high\-volume environments * Advanced optimisation, testing and automation capability * Highly data\-driven with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Confident acting as a senior stakeholder within a cross\-functional environment * Strong communication skills with the ability to influence at multiple levels * Experience within gaming, gambling, fintech, subscription or other regulated digital industries is desirable ### **What’s in it for you:** * Flexible ways of working – home or office, it’s your choice! * £1,000 learning fund * Twice\-yearly bonus (with part of it guaranteed!) * Pension contribution scheme * Private healthcare * Access to thousands of Udemy courses * Invest via the Company Sharesave Scheme * 16 hours paid volunteering time per year * Unlimited holiday ### **About Flutter** Flutter is the world’s leading online sports betting and iGaming operator, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. Our ambition is to change our industry for the better, making use of our significant scale and challenger mentality. By Changing the Game, we believe we can deliver long\-term growth while promoting a positive, sustainable future for the industry. We are well\-placed to do so through the distinctive, global advantages of the Flutter Edge, which gives our brands access to group\-wide benefits to stay ahead of the competition, as well as our clear vision for sustainability through our Positive Impact Plan. Flutter operates a diverse portfolio of leading online sports betting and iGaming brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, tombola, Betfair, MaxBet, Junglee Games and Adjarabet. ### **About Flutter UK \& Ireland** The UK \& Ireland region of Flutter unites some of the biggest brands in the betting and gaming industry; Betfair, Paddy Power, PokerStars, Sky Betting \& Gaming and tombola. At Flutter UK \& Ireland, we strive for the next level and drive innovation to set the pace as leaders, putting our **customers first, always** . We **win together** through team spirit and unparalleled dedication. When we’re **free to be ourselves** , we thrive and unleash our unique talents —creating a culture that empowers our people to **change the game.** We see opportunity everywhere and there is always more to discover... **We’re working to be an inclusive employer. We encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.** **If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.**

title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Nurse – Small Animal** ----------------------------------- **AllPets Veterinary Hospital, Drogheda, Co. Louth** **Full\-time, Permanent** **Salary up to €40,000 DOE** ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ### **A Role Where You Can Learn, Grow and Feel Supported** AllPets Veterinary Hospital is looking for a Veterinary Nurse to join our busy, modern small animal hospital in Drogheda. This is a permanent opportunity within a large, experienced team where learning is part of everyday life and support is always close at hand. Whether you’re newly qualified or bring years of experience, this is a role where you’ll be encouraged to develop your skills, build confidence, and shape a career that suits your interests. ### **A Hospital Environment That Elevates Your Skills** Working in a well\-equipped, high\-standard hospital setting, you’ll be involved across all aspects of nursing \- from theatre and anaesthesia to inpatient care, consults, and client support. For newer graduates, this is an ideal place to start. You’ll be surrounded by experienced vets and nurses who are invested in your development, offering guidance, mentoring, and hands\-on support as you build your confidence in clinical practice. For more experienced nurses, the opportunity here is to take your skills further. With access to advanced equipment, a varied caseload, and colleagues with certifications across multiple disciplines, you’ll have the environment and support to refine your expertise and develop a clinical niche. ### **Develop Your Career, Your Way** We actively support professional development at every stage. If you have a particular area of interest; whether that’s anaesthesia, ECC, medicine, dentistry or beyond; you’ll be encouraged to pursue it. We offer full funding for postgraduate certificates, alongside a strong culture of continuous learning through in\-house training, coaching, and collaboration with highly experienced clinicians. This is a practice where your ambition is supported, not limited. ### **A Supportive and Collaborative Team** AllPets is home to a large, friendly, and highly skilled team, including multiple veterinary surgeons and nurses with additional certifications in areas such as surgery, medicine, dentistry and ECC. You’ll be working in an environment where teamwork is central, communication is open, and everyone plays a valued role in delivering excellent patient care. It’s a place where you can ask questions, share ideas, and feel genuinely supported day to day. ### **A Modern, Well\-Equipped Hospital** Our Drogheda hospital is fully equipped to support high\-quality veterinary care, with digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound, in\-house laboratory equipment, and excellent surgical facilities. We are also proud to be a Gold\-accredited ISFM Cat Friendly Clinic, reflecting our commitment to patient welfare and clinical excellence. ### **A Rota That Works for You** This is a full\-time role, typically based around a 37\.5\-hour working week. Our rota is designed to be fair and sustainable, with a shared weekend commitment and no overnight out\-of\-hours work. We are also open to discussing part\-time or flexible working arrangements to suit your lifestyle and commitments. ### **A Package That Supports You** Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll benefit from a comprehensive package designed to support your wellbeing and career progression. This includes: #### **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave #### **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave * Company sick pay #### **Development** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities #### **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid #### **Additional benefits** * Pawesome benefits – various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Free flu jabs * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) ### ### **Join a Team That Invests in You** If you’re looking for a role where you can develop your skills, feel supported by an experienced team, and build a long\-term career in a progressive hospital environment, AllPets Veterinary Hospital could be the perfect fit. We’d love to hear from you. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
