title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Practitioner \- Small Animal** ------------------------------------------- **Anicare Vets, Blanchardstown, Dublin** ---------------------------------------- **Full\-time, Permanent (Part\-time considered)** ------------------------------------------------- **Salary up to €75,000 DOE** ---------------------------- ### **A Role Where You Can Truly Thrive** This is an opportunity to join a practice where your clinical skills are valued, your development is supported, and your work\-life balance is genuinely respected. At Anicare Blanchardstown, we’re looking for a small animal veterinary surgeon who wants more than just a job \- someone who enjoys being part of a collaborative team, takes pride in delivering high standards of care, and is keen to continue growing professionally in a well\-equipped, modern environment. Whether you’re looking to deepen your clinical interests, develop a niche, or simply work somewhere you feel supported and trusted, this is a role where you can settle in and thrive. ### **A Balanced and Thoughtful Working Pattern** This role is structured around an average 40\-hour working week, designed to provide both strong clinical exposure and protected time to recharge. In practice, your weekly rota will typically be built across 4 days, with some weeks closer to 36 hours and others slightly higher, depending on the weekend rota. This balances out across the rota cycle to an average of 40 hours per week. The weekend rota is fair and clearly structured, averaging approximately 1\.5 weekends in every 6 weeks, and is factored into your overall hours. There are no out\-of\-hours or on\-call requirements, and bank holiday commitments are minimal, usually limited to one or two per year. The overall aim is to provide a sustainable, predictable working pattern that supports both clinical quality and a healthy work\-life balance. ### **A Clinic Designed for Quality Care** Our Blanchardstown practice has recently undergone refurbishment and is equipped to a high standard, allowing you to work cases thoroughly and confidently. You’ll have access to dedicated surgical and dental theatres, digital and dental x\-ray, ultrasound, and a fully equipped in\-house laboratory. We’re particularly proud of our feline\-friendly approach, with separate dog and cat wards and a dedicated cat consult room \- making this an especially appealing environment for anyone with an interest in feline medicine. The caseload is varied, with a healthy mix of medical and surgical work, and excellent scope for someone with a passion for dentistry to really make the most of the facilities available. ### **A Team That Works Together** You’ll be joining a close\-knit and experienced team of four vets, four nurses, and two support staff. Collaboration is a big part of how we work. There is always someone to discuss cases with, share ideas, or simply support each other through a busy day. Within the team, there is strong clinical expertise, including advanced knowledge in cardiology and diagnostic imaging, giving you access to valuable support and learning opportunities. As part of the wider Anicare Veterinary Group and IVC network, you’ll also benefit from broader clinical communities, mentorship, and long\-term career pathways. ### **Who Will Fit In Here** This role would suit a vet with at least a couple of years’ experience in small animal practice who is confident with routine surgery and consultations. It’s not a position suited to a new graduate, as there will be elements of autonomy required. You must be VCI registered and eligible to work in Ireland. We’re looking for someone approachable, team\-oriented, and motivated to continue developing their skills. An interest in feline medicine or dentistry would be a real advantage, but most importantly, you should enjoy good\-quality general practice and working as part of a supportive team. ### **A Package That Supports You Long\-Term – What We Offer** Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll be supported with a comprehensive benefits package designed around your wellbeing and career progression: #### **Work Life Balance \& Employee Wellbeing** * 22 days annual leave * Additional holiday entitlement for your birthday * Paid maternity, paternity, and adoptive leave packages * Cycle to Work Scheme * Company Sick Pay \& Bereavement Leave * Access to EAP \& Wellbeing Centre #### **Career Development** * €1300 annual CPD allowance with 40 hours paid CPD leave * Full funding for any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Opportunities for clinical and leadership progression * VCI and VDS memberships fully covered * Dedicated CARE fund \- €1,100 annually to support exceptional cases #### **Additional Benefits** * Annual Pay Reviews * Employer Pension Contributions * Discounted Veterinary Fees across the IVC Evidensia network * Free Flu Vaccinations * Access to “Pawesome” Benefits – retail and lifestyle discount platform * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) ### **Let’s Have a Conversation** If you’re looking for a role where you can do your best clinical work, feel part of a genuinely supportive team, and enjoy a balanced lifestyle in Dublin, we’d love to hear from you. Get in touch to find out more \- even if you’re just curious, we’re always happy to have an informal chat. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. title: Vice President, Internal Audit - EMEA
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Vice President, Internal Audit \- EMEA**What does a successful VP of Audit do at Fiserv?** At Fiserv, a successful Internal Audit Vice President will guide and oversee various audit engagements and initiatives. You will leverage your extensive experience in auditing and leadership to provide strategic guidance, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and business objectives. **What you will do:** * Execute a risk\-based audit strategy aligned with Fiserv's business goals. * Lead and manage the Internal Audit team, providing strategic oversight and guidance. * Oversee complex audit engagements ensuring compliance with applicable standards. * Provide leadership and guidance to the audit planning process. * Foster strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including regulators. * Ensure compliance with governance, risk management, and control standards. * Drive continuous improvement in audit processes and methodologies. * Acts as an escalation point for complex issues and implement measures to remediate. * Mentor and develop audit staff to promote professional growth. **What you will need to have:** * 12\+ years of experience in audit with 5\+ years leading audit teams. * Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, information systems, or business administration and/or equivalent military experience. * Demonstratable knowledge of Global Internal Auditing Standards and latest audit techniques. * Strong communication skills with a proven ability to build rapport and interact with cross\-functional teams. * Certified Internal Auditor (CIA), Certified Public Accountant (CPA), or equivalent certifications. **What would be great to have:** * Advanced degree in a related field. * Extensive experience in financial services or fintech industry. * Knowledge of global laws, regulations, and standards, particularly in highly regulated environments. * Experience leveraging data analytics within the audit function. Thank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please: * Apply using your legal name * Complete the step\-by\-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable). **Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:** Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law. **Note to agencies:** Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions. **Warning about fake job posts:** Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.
